metadata.name: Bourdain Atlas
credits: Venue pins: community Google My Maps by Reddit user deannd (5-map series)
City/country + dish notes: anthonybourdainworldmap.com (deannd & Peter Keating)
Episode stops: Christine Zhang, bourdain-travel-places (CC BY 4.0)

properties.name: Mount Erebus
properties.note: On the helicopter ride, they also saw Mount Erebus, the southernmost active volcano on Earth.

properties.name: South Pole
properties.note: Tony visited the Polar Plateau. It is 9000 feet above sea level. The altitude and extreme wind is incredibly harsh, dropping to below 50F. The base has the feel of a space station.

properties.name: Cape Royds Adélie Penguin Rookery
properties.note: Tony visited this penguin colony of 2000 breeding pairs. He talked with marine ecologists Dr. David Ainley, who has been studying the colony for 20 years.

properties.name: Galley
properties.note: Tony had a meal with 2 women heavy machine operators.

properties.name: Lake Hoare
properties.note: One of the most oldest and legendary base camps. Everything comes in by helicopter. The manager at the camp is said to be the best cook on the continent. She made grilled pork tenderloin, grilled mahi sticks, shrimp marinated in chili sauce, homemade sourdough bread, roasted beet salad, edamame salad, roasted vegetables.

properties.name: McMurdo Station
properties.note: Largest US base on Antarctica, and a tight knit community of researchers, scientists, carpenters, pilots, machine operators, and others who run the station.

properties.name: Mount Lister
properties.note: Tony went on a helicopter ride with pilot Ryan Skorecki, who flies scientists around the continent to remote areas. He pointed out Mount Lister from the sky.

properties.name: Bar Sur
properties.note: Tony met with a career server named Mario and had drinks and snacks.

properties.name: Buenos Aires Market
properties.note: Cuts of meat for every budget

properties.name: Club GON
properties.note: Tony came to this soccer club to drink beer and eat chorizo with Chef Soledad Nardelli, and watch kitchen staff of 2 rival restaurants play soccer at 1 am

properties.name: Don Carlos
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Argentina's most famous chef, Francis Mallmann. They ate empanadas, grilled beef, and blood sausage

properties.name: Los Talas del Entrerriano
properties.note: Tony met with therapist Marianna and had parrilla, a traditional Argentine meal called achuras, including kidney, chorizo, morcilla.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony met with Nico (lead singer of band Octafonic) to have a meal at a construction site. In Argentina, construction workers often have barbecued beef together at work once a week.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal of khash (stock flavored by roasted-beef bones) with lavash.

properties.name: Restaurant Poloz Mukuch
properties.note: Tony had a meal with some locals: oxtail soup; stewed liver and heart with tomato, onions, and fresh herbs; sheep’s head.

properties.name: At Gayane's
properties.note: Tony had apricot vodka, and trout wrapped and baked in lavash

properties.name: Dargett Craft Beer
properties.note: Tony had beers here with Tech journalist Artur Papayan, developer Harut Martirosyan, and cybersecurity consultant Ruben Muradyan.

properties.name: Dolmama - Armenia's Restaurant
properties.note: "This is what my soul needed,” Tony said as he ate braised lamb shank with rice pilaf; vegetable manti

properties.name: Gum Market
properties.note: Seasonal produce fresh food market. Tony's hosts shop for ingredients to make a dish called ghapama.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony visited Mariam Movsisyan’s grandmother’s family home. They had ghapama (traditional dish consisting of a baked pumpkin or squash stuffed with rice, nuts, raisins, and apricots), Armenian cheese and lavash, hummus.

properties.name: Half Moon
properties.note: A chef Tony calls the pioneer that put Melbourne on the map as the food capital of Australia. Local snapper sashimi, lough oysters, yellowfin tuna with caramelized pork belly and watermelon, freshwater lobster.

properties.name: Town Hall Kebab
properties.note: Tony really loved the kebab he had here

properties.name: Rumi Brunswick
properties.note: Lebanese food. Lamb brains, softshell prawns. Tony's favorite thing he ate in Melbourne: barbecue quail and lamb ribs. He liked the quail so much he ordered a second plate.

properties.name: Royal Mail Hotel, Dunkeld
properties.note: Sustainable restaurant using ingredients grown/raised on site. The chef trained at Mugaritz, a world-class restaurant in the Basques.

properties.name: The Outback
properties.note: Tony camped out, ate kangaroo barbecued on the fire.

properties.name: Golden Century Seafood Restaurant
properties.note: A restaurant many chefs meet at for after shift meals. Mud crab with ginger and shallots, batter fried lobster with Sichuan peppercorns.

properties.name: Dainty Sichuan Hot Pot - CBD
properties.note: "Wonderfully sadomasochistic play between pleasure and pain; between the scorching searing bite of the dried red Sichuan pepper and the cooling more floral relief of the Sichuan flower petal"

properties.name: Di Stasio Citta
properties.note: Tony ate at this chef's country home

visits.title: Down Under: The Wild West of Cooking

properties.name: Imperial Hotel
properties.note: Restauranteur Con Christopoulos owns this place across from the Parliament. They grilled shrimp and meats.

properties.name: Queen Victoria Market
properties.note: Sprawling indoor market of produce, meat, fish, dairy. Tony and his chef friend Paul ate bratwurst for breakfast

properties.name: The Atlantic Restaurant
properties.note: Tony ate at Ondine Restaurant, which is now closed. This is the current restaurant of the same chef, Donovan Cooke. Tony praised the food for using local ingredients.

visits.title: Down Under: The Wild West of Cooking

properties.name: The Restaurant at Lord Dudley
properties.note: "You can drink in the street in this country, like a civilized human being" Tony said to open this episode.

properties.name: Silverton Hotel
properties.note: Meat pies

properties.name: Botanical Hotel
properties.note: Paul Wilson, head chef at the time of this shooting, cooked wagyu beef on a grill

properties.name: Porteño
properties.note: Argentinian grilled meats. Upstairs is the Gardel's Bar where Tony watched some hipster band.

properties.name: Harry's Cafe de Wheels - Darling Harbour
properties.note: Best meat pies. Tony said this is a must visit whenever in Sydney

properties.name: Tetsuya's Restaurant
properties.note: Fancy. But amazing.

properties.name: Chiswick Woollahra
properties.note: Casual restaurant using local ingredients and growing herbs and veggies on site. "A woodburning oven is central to the rustic menu: flatbreads, slow roasted lamb, and grass fed beef."

properties.name: Victor Churchill
properties.note: "One of the most beautiful butcher shops in the world"

properties.name: Harry's Cafe de Wheels - Woolloomooloo
properties.note: Tony had a curried tiger and a ginger beer. It is a curry beef pie topped with "a heaping scoop of mashed potatoes, a volcano crater full of mushy peas, and an eruption of brown gravy.

properties.name: Gut Purbach
properties.note: In the wine country, the chef raises his own sheep to serve at the restaurant

properties.name: Café Sacher Wien
properties.note: "Sachertorte, a staple for the Viennese coffee and pastry culture."

properties.name: Gastwirtschaft Wratschko
properties.note: Traditional Austrian food. Meatballs with gravy and potatoes; tafelspitz (Austrian version of pot au feu with boiled beef shoulder); pan roasted goose breast with quince and red cabbage; beuschel mit serviettenknoedel (cream stew of veal hearts, lungs, liver, and dumplings.

properties.name: Naschmarkt
properties.note: At Naschmarkt, they stopped at this butcher with a small kitchen in the back room to make cordon bleu. Tony and host bought the ingredients in the market

properties.name: Riesen-Hot-Dog Bratwürstel
properties.note: In search for a cheese-filled sausage (kasekreiner), Tony and friends stopped at this hot dog stand

properties.name: Schönbrunn Palace
properties.note: Tony went to the Kris Kringle market in front of the palace. He had a spezialpunsch and goulash from a stand called Wiener Spiralkartoffel

properties.name: Urbanek
properties.note: Cured meats and cheeses in a food market called the Naschmarkt.

properties.name: Weingut & Heuriger Zahel
properties.note: A restaurant with a vineyard attached. Blood sausage, head cheese, bread with schmaltz and onion. Goose liver and onions. Pheasant breast wrapped in speck (proscuitto) and dumplings.

properties.name: Nagorno-Karabakh
properties.note: Tony camped out in the wilderness with Tigran Grigoryan, a war veteran and aspiring politician; Tatul Hakobyan, a journalist; Saro Saryan, an Armenian born in Azerbaijan who fled during the war. They had jingalov hats, or “lavash bread stuffed with greens and onions before being toasted atop a cast-iron sheet” and Armenian barbecue covered in fennel and fresh herbs

properties.name: Membartsho
properties.note: Also known as the "Burning Lake" although it is on a river, not a lake. There are clay offerings and incense burning.

properties.name: Phallus Centre Shop
properties.note: Tony and Darren visited this place about an hour outside of the city. They visited a town in this area that specialzes in Phallus Handicraft and bought a few phallic art pieces (wooden penis)

properties.name: Folk heritage museum restaurant
properties.note: Tony tried yak hide sautéed with garlic, tomato, and peppercorns with Darren Aronofsky and environmentalist Dasho Benji Dorji

properties.name: Menzu Shiri
properties.note: Tony and Darren Aronofsky ate momos (dumplings) here. Could not find the restaurant on a map.

properties.name: Trongsa
properties.note: Tony and Darren had yak butter tea. Tony tried braised pork, pork-bone soup, pepper soup, and rice. Darren tried betelnut which he said made him feel high.

properties.name: Pelela Pass
properties.note: "The gateway to Central Bhutan," Tony describes. He met with Dr. Nawang Norbu and Darren Aronofsky to have yak butter tea, yak jerky with spiced greens, yak cheese, dried chilies, ara infused with Cordyceps, a fungus that kills its caterpillar-larva host.

properties.name: Manacá Restaurante
properties.city: Aldeia Indígena Guarani do São Silverio
properties.note: Bacalao, shrimp with coconut rice

properties.name: Belém
properties.note: A market on the river has fruits, vegetables, and fish found in this area

properties.name: Ver-o-Peso Market
properties.note: Tony tastes many fruits here not found anywhere else. He also has fish called Filhote, a 600 lb. freshwater fish.

properties.name: Bar Da Lora
properties.note: Tony and Leon met with Ivo Faria, the “godfather” of mineiro cuisine at this restaurant inside the Mercado Central. They had beer, liver with jiló (a veggie like eggplant, only green and only edible when ripe).

properties.name: Bar Do Careca
properties.note: A boteco, or neighborhood restaurant that serves beer. Tony met with Eduardo Maya, “self-taught gastronome,” and Luiz Otavio, a beverage tycoon who specializes in cachaça at Cachaça Vale Verde. They had "ox tongue cooked with basil, mint, and pepper" and "feijão tropeiro: beans, manioc flour smoked in cured meats" and fresh eggs.

properties.name: Birosca S2
properties.note: Tony met with a group of women chefs to eat at this restaurant, including Bruna Martins, who owns the restaurant. Bruna only hires women to work there. They had "turkey neck, braised with butter and garlic, served with white beans, topped with kale and pork belly... ox hump pot-roast, served with mashed potatoes and farofa." While there, they had a scare when a drunk man in a stolen car was seen with a gun. The cameraman and producer dove on top of Tony to protect him, to which he said, "you know, you're NOT the secret service!"

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal cooked by Zora Santos, "a practitioner of old school Afro-Mineira cuisine." They had angu (cornmeal and milk cooked for hours), ora pro nobis (a native green, filled with protein), pork ribs.

properties.name: Mercado Central de Belo Horizonte
properties.note: Tony met with chef Leo Paixão, who trained under Joel Robuchon and now runs Glouton. They tried all sorts of fruits and veggies only found in Brazil.

properties.name: Nono - O Rei do Caldo de Mocotó
properties.note: Open very late into the night, this place serves mocotó, "the ultimate in broke-ass, drunk-ass peasant food. Slow-cooked cow’s foot and/ or other bits, tender and tasty, and believe me, one of my favorites, especially at this hour. We opt for the elite version, served with raw quail egg."

properties.name: Inhotim Museum
properties.note: Deep in the jungle, this contemporary art museum built by an eccentric visionary.

properties.name: Barraca Biruta
properties.note: Vendors on the beach: toasty cheese, shrimp on a skewer, quail egg, sugar cane.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Meal on a family farm with a collection of chefs. They had "smoked pig's head with vegetables and chilies, native green beans called andu prepared with bacon and farofa" (toasted cassava flour). Also, "chicken in brown sauce baked in a clay pot, finished with the blood of chickens fresh killed this morning, served with pequi rice, farofa, and of course, cachaca."

properties.name: Barraca Nordestina
properties.note: Tony had lunch at a place called Barraca Nordestina. This is in the wrong location though. He was in the Rochinha favela when he ate chicken feet, black eyed peas, and veggies.

properties.name: Paraguay River
properties.note: Tony took a boat up the river to see New Bordeaux. While on the boat, they ate grilled dorado fish topped with a mango salsa and catfish

properties.name: Adega Cesari
properties.note: Meat, meat, and more meat. Tony and Ottavia ate at this churrascaria.

properties.name: Armazém São Thiago
properties.note: Known to locals as Bar do Gomes, this bar is located in what Tony calls the "most magical neighborhood" in Rio.

properties.name: Bar e Restaurante Urca
properties.note: This bar serves Fried Sardines, seafood soup, and Pasteles

properties.name: Confeitaria Colombo
properties.note: "Please God kill me now. Just strike me dead." Tony said as he sat in an empty cafe. Pastries are good though.

properties.name: Galeto Sat's
properties.note: Tony had barbecued galeto (baby chicken), which is popular for this dish. Tony had this with potato chips, broccoli rice, and farofa.

properties.name: Municipal Market RJ - CADEG
properties.note: Many stands were closed when he visited this food market

properties.name: Rocinha
properties.note: Tony visited this favela where he was tricked into trying a "natural Viagra" by his guide.

properties.name: Uruguay Tent
properties.note: Tony drank beer and caipirinhas and ate a grilled pork chimichurri sandwich on the Ipanema beach while his wife is training for a Brazilian Jiu Jitsu competition

properties.name: Acarajé da Dinha
properties.note: A food stand that always has a line. Tony had acarajé, "a falafel-like wad of crushed black-eyed peas, seasoned with brown brine shrimp and onions, deep-fried until crispy and golden, with chili spice in dendê oil"

properties.name: Bistrô Brazfood Drink Bar
properties.note: Tony had capirinhas, chorizo, and other snacks at this outdoor restaurant.

properties.name: Mercado da Sete Portas
properties.note: Tony met with famous Brazilian artist, Belo Borba in this public market. They ate a fish that can kill you if not prepared properly, served in a spicy stew.

properties.name: Praia do Rio Vermelho
properties.note: Tony relaxed by the beach and had caipirinhas and fried cheese on a stick.

properties.name: Streetside bar in Pelourinho
properties.note: Tony met with Claudio Madureiro and Marília Hughes Guerreiro Costa, at a streetside cafe/bar on a big music and party night in the storied neighborhood of Pelo.

properties.name: Sorriso da Dadá
properties.note: Famous chef in Brazil. Bahian food: Crab salad, Redfish wrapped in banana leaf. seafood coconut stew (Moqueca)

properties.name: Sorveteria da Ribeira
properties.note: Ice cream!

properties.name: Stella Maris
properties.note: Tony went to the beach

properties.name: Bar da Santa
properties.note: Breakfast of coffee and pao na chapa, or grilled bread served with butter, Tony calls it "the local answer to bagel with a schmear"

properties.name: Bar do Mané
properties.note: Mortadella sandwich is famous

properties.name: Beco do Batman
properties.note: Graffiti art for miles

properties.name: Butantan Institute
properties.note: Snakes

properties.name: Cantina e PizzariaCastelões
properties.note: Eat your pizza with a knife and fork for some reason

properties.name: Carlinhos Restaurante
properties.note: Arais sandwiches

properties.name: Choque Cultural
properties.note: Street art gallery

properties.name: D.O.M.
properties.note: Fine dining highlighting Brazilian ingredients

properties.name: Feijoada da Lana
properties.note: National dish of Brazil. Rice, kale, rib, beans, sausage, beef. Peasant food.

properties.name: Hocca Bar - Mercadão
properties.note: Pastel de Bacalhau

properties.name: Hotel Unique
properties.note: Unique rooms, rooftop pool

properties.name: Izakaya Issa
properties.note: Sake and katsu, udon.

properties.name: Lá da Venda
properties.note: Cakes, fruit, jams, breads

properties.name: Love Story
properties.note: Club and nightlife

properties.name: Luiz Fernandes Bar
properties.note: Fried foods

properties.name: Luz Station
properties.note: Architecture

properties.name: Market Paulista
properties.note: Get the mortadella sandwich. His favorite.

properties.name: Mercado Municipal de São Paulo
properties.note: Meats, cheeses, dried fruits, nuts, etc.

properties.name: Mocotó Café
properties.note: Northeast Brazilian food. Tony says put at top of your list

properties.name: Monumento às Bandeiras
properties.note: Monument to explorers

properties.name: Museum of Art of São Paulo Assis Chateaubriand
properties.note: Art museum

properties.name: Open Museum of Urban Art of São Paulo - SP-MAAU
properties.note: Open air street art museum

properties.name: Rua Oscar Freire
properties.note: Fish and fruit market

properties.name: Valadares
properties.note: Fried things. Tony ate the testicles. And capirinhas.

properties.name: Oudongk
properties.note: Tony ate noodles for breakfast at a roadside stand in this town. He then took a lorry (a makeshift traincar made of stacked wood) and stopped at the Soys, a family on a rice farm living in a traditional stilt house. They cooked him chicken with shredded banana flowers, leben, and coriander. Sour beef stew with lemongrass, turmeric, fermented fish, and kantrop leaves. Sticky rice cooked in bamboo, and a dessert made from palm sugar.

properties.name: Krong Kampot
properties.note: Somewhere between Angkor Wat and this port city, Tony stopped at a roadside restaurant which served freshly made spring rolls and noodles.

properties.name: Ta Eou Restaurant
properties.note: Tony had lunch with Mu Sochua, an ethnic Cambodian-Chinese member of Parliament.

properties.name: Crab Market
properties.note: Tony met Madame Kek, a humanitarian, in this seaside town that used to be a resort town for rich people before 1970 and is now in ruins. They went to the crab market and bought crabs, shrimp, rice, and rambutan.

properties.name: Central Market
properties.note: Tony was upset that this market he went to 10 years ago was now more commercialized. He went to a smaller local and less touristy market and had Ka Tiew, a noodle soup (they did not say where this was)

properties.name: Hang Meas Hotel & Restaurant
properties.note: The only hotel in the town

properties.name: Phnom Penh
properties.note: Tony described Cambodian food as light on meat, heavy on vegetables, fruits, and seafood. Visited many food stands.

properties.name: Phnom Penh Night Market
properties.note: Tony had another bowl of noodle soup here

properties.name: Phnom Penh Shooting Range Cambodia
properties.note: A shooting range with snacks

properties.name: Special Happy Pizza
properties.note: Tony had a "happy pizza" which included "powerful herbs"

properties.name: Raffles Grand Hotel d'Angkor
properties.note: Royal Cambodian meal

visits.title: Eating on the Edge of Nowhere

properties.name: Angkor Wat
properties.note: Tony called it "endless"

visits.title: Eating on the Edge of Nowhere

properties.name: Cabane à Sucre Au Pied De Cochon
properties.note: Tony had a meal here that started with a tower of maple desserts. It had a lot of courses which would be too many to list!

properties.name: Chef Martin Picard's home
properties.note: Tony ate beaver at Chef Martin Picard's home.

properties.name: Au Pied de Cochon
properties.note: One of Tony's favorite restaurants. Specialize in Foie Gras and pork

properties.name: Bistro M Sur Masson
properties.note: Tony met with journalist Patrick Lagacé at this restaurant (closed in 2016). They discussed "pastagate" which is the scandal that happened when a restaurant got in trouble for using the word "pasta" which is Italian

properties.name: Canadiens de Montréal
properties.note: Tony went with the chefs to watch the Montreal Canadiens play the Carolina Hurricanes at the stadium

properties.name: Gare Centrale Train Station
properties.note: Tony, along with chefs Fred Morin and David McMillian, traveled by train to Quebec City. The chefs brought along a gourmet meal including foie gras and truffles.

properties.name: Joe Beef
properties.note: Fred Morin and Dave McMillan's restaurant (Tony's good friends)

properties.name: La Banquise
properties.note: Poutine

properties.name: Liverpool House
properties.note: Sister restaurant to Joe Beef. Tony met with the chefs and ate Pakistani food by Chef Omar. "Butter chicken crab; little eggplants braised with anar seeds, pomegranate; mushrooms; rabbit korma; fingerlings with fenugreek and fennel; donkey nahari; goat biryani; and more.

properties.name: St-Viateur Bagel Shop
properties.note: Bagels are sweeter and crispier than NYC here

properties.name: Vinet Park
properties.note: Tony watched a game of "shinny hockey," a pickup game of street hockey played on the ice. Meanwhile they ate Choucroute garnie a l'alsacienne (lots of meats and sausages piled on a plate).

properties.name: Wilensky's Light Lunch Inc
properties.note: Open since 1932, Tony had the "special,” a beef bologna and salami sandwich with mustard heated on a sandwich press, an egg cream.

properties.name: Appetite For Books
properties.note: Independent Bookstore

properties.name: BAR - Big in Japan
properties.note: Drinks

properties.name: Beauty's Restaurant
properties.note: Old school breakfast diner

properties.name: Brasserie Capri
properties.note: Canadian pub

properties.name: Brasserie T! Montréal - Quartier des spectacles
Club Chasse et Pêche
properties.note: Fancy

properties.name: Club Social
properties.note: Coffee

properties.name: Dominion Square Tavern
properties.note: One of the oldest taverns in Montreal. Moules frites and great cocktails

properties.name: Fromagerie du Marché Atwater
properties.note: Cheesemonger

properties.name: Gibeau Orange Julep
properties.note: Muscle cars and food

properties.name: Grumman'78
properties.note: Tacos

properties.name: Jean Talon Market
properties.note: One of 2 main farmer's markets

properties.name: L'Express
properties.note: French Bistro

properties.name: Lachine Canal
properties.note: Park overlooking the canal

properties.name: Restaurant Marven's
properties.note: Old World Greek food

properties.name: Rotisserie Romados
properties.note: Portuguese roast chicken.

properties.name: Schwartz's Deli - Sandwiches - Steaks
properties.note: Best pastrami

properties.name: Chafe's Landing Restaurant
properties.note: Tony had fish & chips with Dale Jarvis, Intangible Cultural Heritage Development Officer for the Heritage Foundation of Newfoundland and Labrador.

properties.name: Christian's Bar
properties.note: Tony met with chefs Fred Morin and Dave McMillan. They had a fried bologna cube and Newfoundland‘s very own Screech Rum.

properties.name: Jack Axes
properties.note: Tony, Fred, and Dave threw axes and had a cheese plate, an assortment of patés, and pickled pork tongue.

properties.name: Raymonds Restaurant
properties.note: Tony, Fred Morin, and Dave McMillan had a seafood tower of "razor clams, snow crab, mussels, capelin, whelks, and sea urchin," boquerones; scallop crudo with smoked roe; tuna prepared with berries; heart of caribou tartare with alder and chanterelle mushrooms; charred whelk with crispy cod sounds (fried fish bladders); cod filet with cod liver canelé, partridge profiteroles, Jiggs dinner (a boiled dish of meat and root veggies) reinvented as a pasta dish, chanterelle ice cream.

properties.name: Red Indian Lake
properties.note: Tony went with a group of chefs to a place up near the lake to eat some hearty cod stew, various terrines (duck, rabbit, guinea hen, boar), jellied foie gras, fire-roasted moose shoulder and leg, ribs braised with red wine, local boletus, and chanterelle mushrooms.

properties.name: The Big R Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and a group of chefs had fried bologna which Tony called "awesome," fried clam strips, liver and onions, pan-fried cod tongues, Jiggs dinner, french fries.

properties.name: L'Affaire est Ketchup
properties.note: The restaurant name means "Everything's Cool" in local slang. They had razor clams, a cream of haddock roe, Coqui St. Jacques, terrine of foie gras, head cheese with cassis mustard, truffled sweetbreads, goose hearts persillade, salt cod.

properties.name: Le Continental
properties.note: Old style restaurant. Lots of tableside service including caesar salad, beef tartare, and shrimp cocktail. David McMillian had the filet mignon, Fred had the Scampi Newberg, Tony had the Dover Sole

properties.name: Sucrerie de la Montagne
properties.note: Restaurant specializing in maple syrup

properties.name: Ice Fishing on the St. Lawrence
properties.note: Tony visited in the winter time and met up with chefs Fred Morin and David McMillan of Joe Beef. They went ice fishing on the St. Lawrence River, then ate a fancy meal in the small cottage on the ice.

properties.name: Saint Lawrence River
properties.note: There is a permanent wave here so people surf!

properties.name: Sooke Harbour House Hotel & Resort
properties.note: Restaurant with local seasonal ingredients, and a hotel

properties.name: Agave Y Aguacate
properties.note: Mexican takeout

properties.name: Altona Kabob
properties.note: Grilled meats with rice and fixin's

properties.name: Bairrada Churrasqueira Grill
properties.note: Portuguese food

properties.name: Beast Restaurant
properties.note: Seasonal/local food

properties.name: California Sandwiches
properties.note: Italian sandwiches

properties.name: Carousel Bakery
properties.note: Pea meal style bacon sandwich

properties.name: Cold Tea
properties.note: Hard to find bar, it's behind the dim sum stand

properties.name: Edulis Restaurant
properties.note: Local ingredients

properties.name: Evergreen Brick Works
properties.note: Sandwiches

properties.name: Forestview Chinese Restaurant
properties.note: Dim sum

properties.name: Kensington Market
properties.note: Neighborhood with burger joints, drinks, shopping, etc.

properties.name: Le Swan
properties.note: Tony recommended the Black Hoof, but the restaurant is closed now. This is the chef's new restaurant

properties.name: Porchetta & Co.
properties.note: Porchetta sandwiches

properties.name: Poutini’s House of Poutine
properties.note: Poutine

properties.name: Ronnie's Local 069
properties.note: Beer on a patio

properties.name: Sneaky Dee's
properties.note: Punk shows

properties.name: SPIN Toronto
properties.note: Ping pong bar

properties.name: St. Lawrence Market
properties.note: Oldest market in the city with food stands

properties.name: The Burger's Priest
properties.note: Burger place. There are a few locations

properties.name: The Drake Hotel
properties.note: More affordable option; rooftop lounge, underground performance space, good food

properties.name: The Famous Owl of Minerva
properties.note: Korean food

properties.name: The Residences
properties.note: Ritz Carlton

properties.name: Tosho Knife Arts Inc.
properties.note: Knife shop

properties.name: WVRST
properties.note: German food and beer

properties.name: Cioppino's Mediterranean Grill
properties.note: Modern Italian food with local ingredients

properties.name: JAPADOG
properties.note: Japanese flavors with hot dogs. Nori with daikon and teriyaki hot dog.

properties.name: Save On Meats
properties.note: Cheap burgers

properties.name: Tojo's Restaurant
properties.note: Local seafood cooked in Japanese recipes. Tempura fried zucchini blossoms stuffed with fresh scallops. Dungeness crab, daikon with pepper, mustard miso dressing, Japanese celery.

properties.name: Vij's
properties.note: Indian food

properties.name: Angelmo
properties.note: Tony ate fresh uni and seafood at this market

properties.name: Donde El Gordito
properties.note: The name translates to "Where's the little fat guy." Corvina, a local fish, is cooked in a creamy king crab salpicon

properties.name: Café Caribe
properties.note: Coffee con Piernes - beautiful women in tight outfits serve you coffee

properties.name: El Hoyo
properties.note: Known for their pork leg (pernil). A drink called the tornado has wine and frozen pineapple juice

properties.name: Fuente Alemana
properties.note: Translates to "The German Fountain," a 60-year old institution serving the "lomito" which is a pile of pork and avocado.

properties.name: La Vega Chica

properties.name: Vega Central
properties.note: Food market, founded 100+ years ago. Staggering abundance of local produce, meat, fish

properties.name: Sandwichería SIBARÍTICO
properties.note: Completo hot dog: a 12 inch hot dog covered with sauerkraut, tomatoes, guacamole, topped with mayo.

properties.name: Don Vittorio
properties.note: Typical Chilean favorites: Abalone, razor clams with cheese, fried conger eel, a seafood stew called mariscal.

properties.name: La Playa
properties.note: Tony and his guide had pisco sours.

properties.name: Mercado Puerto
properties.note: Fresh seafood close to the source.

properties.name: Li Qun Roast Duck Restaurant
properties.note: Roast duck

properties.name: Liubiju Pickles
properties.note: Pickled veggies

properties.name: Noodle Loft
properties.note: Watch expert noodlemakers make noodles!

properties.name: Dave’s Oasis
properties.note: Expat bar

properties.name: Huangcheng Laoma Hot Pot
properties.note: Spicy Sichuan food

properties.name: Laoyikang Tea House
properties.note: Outdoor tea house

properties.name: Tiantian Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and Eric ate at this sichuan restaurant, and Eric said the spice was "challenging" They had pickled chicken feet, la zi ji (spicy chicken), mapo tofu (Tony's favorite), tong jiao yu (Sichuan green-peppercorn fish)

properties.name: Xiaomingtang Dandantian Shuimian
properties.note: For Tony and Eric Ripert's first place, they had dan dan tian shui mian.(spicy noodles) from this place.

properties.name: Yu Zhilan Restaurant
properties.note: Not sure if this restaurant is still here. Tony and Eric met with Fuchsia Dunlop, a James Beard award-winning writer and cook who specializes in Chinese cuisine. They had "lily buds scented with rosebuds, thinly sliced beef shank with chili oil, tea tree mushrooms blanched and lightly stir-fried, prawns served cold with homemade noodles dressed with natural vegetable extracts, noodles hand-cut with duck-egg yolks, braised sea cucumber."

properties.name: Zhaoji Laopu
properties.note: Manchu style dinner - imperial cuisine of the Qing Dynasty

properties.name: Qingshui River
properties.note: Tony and Eric took a train out to Long Teng Dong Jie to eat a meal with Chen Zhong, a filmmaker.

properties.name: Around the World
properties.note: Classic Russian restaurant. With vodka.

properties.name: Dasong Dumplings
properties.note: This place is a 24-hour restaurant known for their dumplings

properties.name: Fox Pub
properties.note: Tony's handler is from Belfast, and owns this pub

properties.name: Quick-fried Tripe Zhang
properties.note: Beijing restaurant specializing in dishes made with cow and sheep stomach

properties.name: Shazhutian
properties.note: Large bustling restaurant hall serving all kinds of pork preparations

properties.name: Zhangfei Braised Pork
properties.note: Pork centric belly warming foods to warm you up. And beer

properties.name: Donghe Barbecue Yabuli Branch
properties.note: Tony had grilled meats on a stick from a mobile food stand

properties.name: Aberdeen Fish Market Yee Hope Seafood Restaurant
properties.note: Seafood market. Wear your rain boots!

properties.name: Chan Chi Kee Cutlery Company
properties.note: Tony bought a cleaver here. The neighborhood is a restaurant supply area.

properties.name: Hui Lau Shan
properties.note: Cold mango drinks

properties.name: Joy Hing Roasted Meat
properties.note: Roast goose, roast pork

properties.name: Kin's Kitchen 留家廚房
properties.note: Sauteed chicken/prawns

properties.name: Lamcombe Seafood Restaurant
properties.note: Ocean view restaurant

properties.name: Lamma Island
properties.note: Island with no cars. Only boats and bikes

properties.name: Mak An Kee Noodle
properties.note: Wontons, noodles

properties.name: Mandarin Oriental, Hong Kong
properties.note: Fancy hotel

properties.name: OZONE
properties.note: 118 floors up with an excellent view with tapas. Fancy

properties.name: Pier 3 Bar & Restaurant
properties.note: Also called New Discovery, cheap beer and a great view

properties.name: Racks City
properties.note: Bar with pool tables

properties.name: Simpson Sin Tailors
properties.note: Tony got a tailor made gangsta suit made here

properties.name: Tim Ho Wan
properties.note: Michelin starred modern dim sum restaurant.

properties.name: Lianglukou Hotpot
properties.note: By the end of this meal of painfully spicy Sichuan hot pot (dog tail; tripe, or “thousand-layer stomach”; tofu skin, seaweed, various meats and fishes, intestines), Eric said he couldn't think anymore (because of the spice). Tony enjoyed the meal and watching Eric squirm.

properties.name: Leshan Giant Buddha
properties.note: Tony and Eric took a boat ride to see the largest stone buddha in the world, built in 713.

properties.name: Fernando's Restaurant
properties.note: Portuguese Restaurant serving grilled chorizo; clams with garlic, white wine, cilantro, malagueta peppers; grilled sardines; suckling pig

properties.name: Chun Restaurant
properties.note: "Classic, homestyle Shanghai meals," Tony described of this restaurant that is a favorite of Yao Minji, chief feature reporter for SHINE. No menu, no waiting list. Tony had you bao xia (wok-fried shrimp); hong shao rou (braised pork belly); yan du xian (clay pot soup with bean curd stock, pork belly, tofu, and bamboo)

properties.name: Constellation Bar
properties.note: Tony and Thomas Yao pregamed here before crashing a wedding. They had moscow mules

properties.name: Dali Shijiagong
properties.note: Street market every month.

properties.name: Di Shui Dong
properties.note: "Hacker turned entrepreneur Thomas Yao" met with Tony at this place, where they ate ziran paigu (cumin-rubbed ribs).

properties.name: Erguang Wonton (Zhaozhou Road)
properties.note: Tony's dinner guest, English teacher Bill Wang, says this is the best place to get wontons. Minced pork, bok choy, rice wine in a wonton dough, served with a mixture of soy sauce, vinegar, chili sauce, sesame oil, and peanut butter. "YOU NEED THIS," Tony exclaims.

properties.name: Fuchun Xiaolong
properties.note: Tony had xiao long bao (soup dumplings), fried pork chop with brown sauce with Professor Zhou Lin, Dean at Shanghai Jiao Tong University

properties.name: Jade Buddha Temple
properties.note: A monastery with a public vegetarian restaurant where monks cook.

properties.name: Mingliu Hair Salon
properties.note: Getting your hair washed is like a spa treatment in Taiwan.

properties.name: The House of Roosevelt
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Roosevelt China Investments President Tim Tse. They had French oysters with champagne, New Zealand shrimp, Australian Wagyu beef

properties.name: Wujiang Road
properties.note: Night food market with lots of street food

properties.name: Yang's Dumpling
properties.note: Popular fried soup dumplings. Tofu Beef Noodle soup

properties.name: Yangcheng Lake Hairy Crab
properties.note: Lots of internal tourism for these crabs which is prized for being the best in China

properties.name: Apomac
properties.note: Specialize in Macanese food.

properties.name: Leiteria I Son
properties.note: Chinese dish called Steamed Milk Pudding

properties.name: Macau Military Club Restaurant
properties.note: Tony had a gin & tonic with a priest

properties.name: The 8 Restaurant (Grand Lisboa)
properties.note: Michelin starred dim sum restaurant. Roasted chicken with honey lime sauce; dumplings in the shape of goldfish; Roasted pork belly; fish ball soup; crab claw steamed, served with ginger, white wine, egg white

properties.name: Zhuji Pie Chinese Type Fast-food
properties.note: Meat filled dumpling/pies

properties.name: Tianxia Di Yiwei
properties.note: Fast fry style restaurant. Bamboo shoot, egg noodles, sea snails, fried oysters, crispy cod filet, 3-cup chicken (classic Taiwanese dish)

properties.name: Queareparaenamorart
properties.note: This was a restaurant called Queareparaenamorarte. It is closed now.

properties.name: La Puerta Falsa
properties.note: Tony met with Hector Abad, a writer. They ate tamales with chicken and pork belly.

properties.name: Paloquemao
properties.note: A large market, where Tony met with a guide to have some fresh carrot and orange juice. They also ate corn arepas. In the back corner of the fish market, they had Caldo de Costilla, a traditional breakfast soup from the Andean region, which has beef short ribs in a broth with potatoes and scallions.

properties.name: Tábula
properties.note: Tony met with chef and musician Tomas Rueda, and they had crab salad, handmade pasta, stuffed with labneh cheese with a chorizo sauce; braised osso buco (with beef shank) over vegetables, wine and broth in a wood-fired oven.

properties.name: Cecilia restaurant
properties.note: Seafood. Tony ate a dish with turtle and turtle eggs, coconut milk, garlic, rosemary

properties.name: La Cevichería
properties.note: Exotic ingredients with Caribbean flavors

properties.name: Plaza De Mercado De Cartagena
properties.note: Fresh fish, outdoor produce market

properties.name: Miraflores
properties.note: The plane landed here in Miraflores, a jungle outpost in the southern province of Guaviare in the Amazon Forest Preserve. Tony talked with a guide there about the cocaine trade of the past.

properties.name: Goat vendor
properties.note: Tony met Chef Juan Pablo Mayorga in this town in La Guajira. They ate Frichi, a traditional dish from the Wayuu people, includes the offal of the goat. They then rode ATVs.

properties.name: Mayapo beaches
properties.note: Tony rested on the beach after crashing on his ATV, had beer, and ate at a restaurant called Mar Azul (which I could not find on the map). They had cazuela, which is a fish chowder made with lobster, fish, shrimp, clams, and conch.

properties.name: Plaza Minorista José María Villa
properties.note: Food market with tons of produce. They had empanadas.

properties.name: La Vanguardia Airport
properties.note: "On first inspection, this is an airplane boneyard, where unwanted props from Romancing the Stone corrode artfully. But in reality, this sleepy hangar is an important gateway to the more impenetrable parts of the country," Tony says.

properties.name: Sevicheria Guapi
properties.note: The Cali version of ceviche: cooked shrimp, slathered in mayonnaise, ketchup, and Worcestershire sauce; rock mollusk with rice; steamed shrimp with green tomatoes; beer; sugar cane (viche), plantains.

properties.name: Social Club Los Amigos
properties.note: Tony met with a band called On the Tropica to play tejo, a game involving "alcohol and explosives." They also ate a Colombian picada, "a huge selection of fried pork, pork rib, steak, cassava, potatoes, and deep fried plantain."

properties.name: Tonys oyster shack
properties.note: Tony had seafood here

properties.name: KONOBA MONDO
properties.note: Tony ate at this truffle restaurant

properties.name: Boskinac
properties.note: One of the best restaurants in the country. They had octopus stewed in white wine and tomatoes. Slow cooked lamb with hand rolled pasta

properties.name: Batelina
properties.note: Tony ate at the MasterChef Croatia winner's restaurant, which serves seafood that are usually seen as "trash fish" and fishermen would cook with it

properties.name: Avenida de Bélgica
properties.note: Mercado Egido, a fresh produce market

properties.name: Cafeteria a la BBQ
properties.note: Tony ate with internationally renowned author Leonardo Padura. They had fried pork and plantains with flan made out in a cut down beer can.

properties.name: El Aljibe
properties.note: Specialty is roast chicken in orange sauce with rice and black beans

properties.name: El Templete
properties.note: Basque chef from Spain. Marmitako (seafood stew)

properties.name: Fábrica de Arte Cubano
properties.note: Nightclub, performance space, and art gallery highlighting artists and DJs from around the world.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate at local's home, she is a journalist for Havana Times. They had pork marinated in garlic, onion, and sour orange, served with rice.

properties.name: Restaurante Tien Tan
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Jon Lee Anderson, a writer for the New Yorker. They had steamed dumplings and "Sichuan chicken that is about as Sichuan as I am"

properties.name: Santy Pescador
properties.note: Tony had sushi with Hugo Cancio, a Cuban born entrepreneur

properties.name: Paladar Los Amigos
properties.note: Small restaurant. Tony had Masa de cerdo (pork) and pork cutlet served with yucca, rice, and beans

properties.name: Caribeño Restaurante
properties.note: Could not find this restaurant on the map. Tony had dorado, rice, beans, plantains, and rum.

properties.name: Café Savoy
properties.note: Tony met a guide here that promised to take him to eat some pho.

properties.name: Kavárna V Sedmém Nebi
properties.note: Tony had some beers here

properties.name: Pho Quynh Anh
properties.note: After lots of delays where the crew could not secure permission to film, they finally got to eat at this Vietnamese restaurant in Little Hanoi.

properties.name: Pivovar U Medvídků
properties.note: Traditional Czech restaurant

properties.name: Pivovarský klub
properties.note: Beer from regional producers,

properties.name: Wenceslas Square
properties.note: Tony's continuous search for "meat in tube form" leads him to this sausage stand. It's a fast food place, so his chef friend is surprised how much he likes it.

properties.name: Kisangani
properties.note: Tony ate at a BBQ kiosk in the city where he had goat stew.

properties.name: Kisangani Railway Station
properties.note: Tony visited this rail station that the Belgians built during their occupation of the Congo. About 200 volunteers still work at the station to keep it running. Tony took a ride on the train

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony visited the Wagenia tribe which live in this region. He had steamed tigerfish limboke: steamed in a banana leaf.

properties.name: Yangambi
properties.note: National Institute for Agronomic Study of the Belgian Congo is located here and Tony met with a man who works to save the library of thousands of books here.

properties.name: Congo River
properties.note: Tony had a dream to travel on the Congo River. He cooked some coq au vin on the boat. Later he and crew were stuck here due to technical issues with transportation

properties.name: Fatimata Restaurant
properties.note: This restaurant is supposed to be in Goma, Rwanda, called Village Fatimata. It specializes in Congolese cuisine like grilled piri piri chicken and gugali. This is the only restaurant with the same name, cannot find it in Goma.

properties.name: Funchs Vinstue
properties.note: Tony tried a local drink called Gammel Dansk (Danish bitters)

properties.name: John's Hotdog Deli
properties.note: Tony had the deluxe: sausage made with bacon and garlic, homemade mustard, onions pickled in beer.

properties.name: Noma
properties.note: Tony visited Chef Rene Redzepi at this restaurant, which is known to many as "the best restaurant in the world." Tony came to Denmark solely to visit this restaurant.

properties.name: Tivoli Gardens
properties.note: The second oldest amusement park in the world. Tony and Rene visited and rode on the ferris wheel.

properties.name: El Cabito
properties.note: Gorgeous seaside restaurant. Tony had beers, grilled shrimp, conch and cuttlefish salad

properties.name: Barra Payán
properties.note: Payan Especial: Pork with tomato, mustard, and cheese, heated in a sandwich press. A drink made with orange juice and condensed milk.

properties.name: Boca Chica Beach
properties.note: Tony had Pescado Frito at D'Wendy Sabor, one of the food stands on the beach

properties.name: Lechonera El Monumento
properties.note: Roast pig located across a monument to Trujillo. They break down the pig with a machete.

properties.name: Mesón de Bari
properties.note: Tony had empanadas and longaniza with Dominican Republic

properties.name: El Pez Volador
properties.note: Encebollado, a yellowfin tuna specialty of the region, a fish stew. Tony describes the place as, "A simple food stall in a nondescript corner in Downtown Guayaquil."

properties.name: La Guatita
properties.note: Tony ate tripe soup. His guide had a fish stew with plantains (casuela).

properties.name: Market
properties.note: Tony had black clams, a regional specialty. It was made into a ceviche with shrimp, red onion, cilantro, tomato, and roasted corn. Sprinkled with fried banana chips

properties.name: Pelicano Restaurante.
properties.note: Tony had fresh seafood on the beach

properties.name: Esquina de la Ronda
properties.note: This restaurant no longer exists, but it was a traditional Ecuadorian restaurant

properties.name: Picateria el Hueco
properties.note: Tony tries cuy, which is a local specialty made from roasted guinea pig

properties.name: Sana Sana Las Palmeras
properties.note: Tony ate grilled meats from a food stand right outside this market.

properties.name: Abou Tarek
properties.note: A 1000 year old dish of rice, pasta, black lentils, chickpeas, and fried onions. Served with a tomato cumin hot sauce and a vinegar/garlic sauce to drizzle.

properties.name: Al Rifai Roastery محمصة الرفاعي‎
properties.note: Kofta, sausages, baba ganoush, salad

properties.name: Attaba Square
properties.note: Tony had a breakfast called fooll from a food cart. Fooll is breakfast made of beans and eggs that is served with a flat bread.

properties.name: Khan el-Khalili
properties.note: Oldest spice market in the Middle East

properties.name: Kategna Restaurant
properties.note: Tony met with chef Marcus Samuelsson and his wife Maya. They had beyaynetu, a platter of various stews including goman (sauteed greens), shiro wat (chickpea stew), tikil gomen (sauteed white cabbage); all served with injera bread.

properties.name: Local Tej Bet
properties.note: Tony, Marcus, and Maya had tej, a light alcoholic drink made of fermented barley and honey

properties.name: Mesi Grocery store/መጠጥ መሸጫ
properties.note: Tony had shekla tibs, which are chunks of fried beef or lamb served in a clay pot with Ethiopian skateboarders and Marcus Samuelsson.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.city: Southern Nations Nationalities and People's Region
properties.note: Tony, Marcus, and Maya went to this small village where Maya grew up. They were served coffee. Later they had a feast where they barbecued 2 goats.

properties.name: Jaskan grilli
properties.note: Drunken late night munchies brought Tony and his host to this sausage stand.

properties.name: Kafe Moskova
properties.note: The owner called it a Soviet bar. They ate blood sausage that someone brought, and drank vodka.

properties.name: Spårakoff Tram Tour
properties.note: "The only tram in the world licensed to sell booze." Tony drank a beer on one.

properties.name: Cafe & Pub Päämaja
properties.note: Tony had a pork stew and listened to folk music

properties.name: Arcachon Bay
properties.note: Tony had his first fresh oyster out of the sea here, and he says it was life-changing

properties.name: Boulangerie Patisserie Vincent Chérin
properties.note: The Bourdain brothers bought sausage and cheese for a picnic on the beach

properties.name: Café Français
properties.note: Steak frites

properties.name: Cap Ferret
properties.note: The Bourdains used to play on this beach as kids

properties.name: Clarens Sarl
properties.note: Tony and his brother Chris ate some croissants/bread here

properties.name: Le Bistrot du Port
properties.note: Fish stew

properties.name: Restaurant Chez Yvette
properties.note: Tete de veau (Veal sweetbreads, cheeks, etc)

properties.name: Waffle Factory Bordeaux
properties.note: Gaufre waffles

properties.name: Le Rendez Vous
properties.note: Ludo's mentor is chef at this restaurant. Tony and Ludo ate, escargot, Beef bourguignon, cheese

properties.name: Bar du Marché
properties.note: At 8 am, the men gather and eat charcuterie, bread, and cheese and drink red wine

properties.name: Grain de Vanille
properties.note: Chef Olivier Roellinger's bakery specializing with salted butter caramels, and sand cookies

properties.name: Aiguille des Grands Montets
properties.note: Tony and Eric Ripert went skiing here in the French Alps, which Tony describes as a place he fears, but is allowing Eric to have "payback" for the torture Eric endured in Sichuan China.

properties.name: Boucherie du Mont Blanc - Chamonix (Francia)
properties.note: Eric Ripert met with Maurice Guillot, Eric's mentor at this butcher/cheese shop. They bought beef tenderloin to make steak au poivre, tomme de chevre, and saucisson.

properties.name: ChaChaCha
properties.note: Eric Ripert and mentor Maurice Guillot went to this wine & spirits store to purchase champagne, white burgundy, and cognac.

properties.name: Crémerie du Glacier
properties.note: "A special place at the bottom of the mountain," describes Tony. "croute with morilles, and cream, lots of cream....farcante: a big, heavy, albeit thoroughly delicious loaf of potato, bacon, dried fruit, and cream slowly steamed in a bain marie."

properties.name: Restaurant Plan Joran
properties.note: While skiing in the French Alps, Tony and Eric Ripert stopped here. They had pan-seared foie gras, seared veal loin with a wild mushroom sauce and vegetable medley, a cheese course.

properties.name: Rungis International Market
properties.note: Large food market

properties.name: Paul Bocuse Restaurant
properties.note: Tony said it had always been his dream to eat here. Paul Bocuse is considered the father of modern French cuisine. They ate soupe aux truffes noires V.G.E. (black-truffle soup named after former French President Valéry Giscard d’Estaing); loup en croûte feuilletée (sea bass in pastry crust); choron (béarnaise or hollandaise sauce made with tomato purée); pot-au-feu (French beef stew); lièvre à la royale (roasted hare).

properties.name: Chez Pépin
properties.note: Jacques does a demo making an omelet.

properties.name: The Foie Gras Farm
properties.note: Tony visited the duck farm

properties.name: La Galline
properties.note: Melon and prosciutto

properties.name: Bouchon Comptoir Brunet
properties.note: Bill Buford called this "a very male tradition," called the Franc Machon. They ate Blanquette de Veau (slowly stewed neck and shoulder of veal with mushrooms served over rice); bread, Beaujolais wine.

properties.name: Café Comptoir Abel
properties.note: Tony ate here with Chef Boulud and Bill Buford, a former fiction editor at The New Yorker. They had quenelle de brochet en gratin maison (gratin dumplings)

properties.name: L'Institut Restaurant
properties.note: One of the nation's greatest culinary schools, started by Paul Bocuse. Tony and Daniel Boulud tried the poulet en vessie (chicken cooked inside a pig’s bladder).

properties.name: Reynon
properties.note: Tony ate sabodet (sausage made from pig’s head, tongue, and beef) with Chef Georges Reynon and Chef Daniel Boulud.

properties.name: Café de l'Abbaye
properties.note: Tony and his best friend Eric Ripert ate at this outdoor cafe.

properties.name: Chez Georgiana
properties.note: Tony ate at this restaurant with Eric Ripert and many women in the restaurant business, who discussed how it is to be a female chef in the male-dominated business.

properties.name: L'Emporte Pièce
properties.note: Grilled sandwiches with cheese

properties.name: Le Bar de la Plaine
properties.note: Tony, Eric, and Gilles Rof (local sports writer) played foosball here

properties.name: Le Petit Nice Passedat
properties.note: Marseilles' only Michelin starred restaurant when this was filmed. They had bouillabaisse and a cheese course that Bourdain was obsessed with

properties.name: Restaurant Fémina
properties.note: Restaurant known for their couscous. Tony had the royale, which is served with veggies, merguez sausage, chicken, chickpeas, lamb, and meatballs.

properties.name: The Champ De Mars
properties.note: Tony and Eric had drinks at this bar with a local filmmaker & sports writer, Gilles Rof

properties.name: U Mio Paese
properties.note: Tony and Eric had some fantastic charcuterie at a patio table outside of this charcuterie shop

properties.name: Saint Pierre and Miquelon
properties.note: Tony, Fred, and Dave went to this island to visit Maïté Legasse, home cook and director of Ma P’tite Cocotte. They had sea urchin pâté, stuffed squid over rice, braised halibut, local cheese, tart with local baked apples and blackberries.

properties.name: Peloton de gendarmerie de haute montagne (PGHM)
properties.note: Eric's dream as a kid was to work as a park ranger, so they met with a lady named Sonia Popoff who does this for a living. "She's who you call when things are really bad," says Tony. They rescue skiiers who injure themselves on the mountain.

properties.name: Relais des Grands Crus
properties.note: Truck stop restaurant with great food.

properties.name: Boucherie Chevaline
properties.note: Tony and guide bought proscuitto and sausage here

properties.name: Maison Paillot
properties.note: Tony and Ludo picked a lamb and chefs cooked it for them here and served it to them on the sidewalk table.

properties.name: Noyers
properties.note: Tony visited a farm where he fed a baby lamb by bottle.

properties.name: Troisgros
properties.note: Chefs Michel and Cesar Troisgros carry on the family tradition of nouvelle cuisine. Tony had salmon in sorrel sauce, a dish that he says changed the world.

properties.name: 11 Place de la Madeleine
properties.note: Food truck serving burgers. They have a couple brick & mortar locations too

properties.name: Au Petit Fer à Cheval
properties.note: Coffee

properties.name: Berthillon Glacier
properties.note: Ice cream

properties.name: Café de la Mairie
properties.note: Sidewalk cafe

properties.name: Cantada II
properties.note: Absinthe fountain

properties.name: Cantine California
properties.note: Burgers, tacos, cupcakes

properties.name: Coquelicot
properties.note: Bakery and cafe

properties.name: Du Pain et des Idées
properties.note: Many consider this the best bakery in Paris. ONLY breads.

properties.name: E.DEHILLERIN
properties.note: Kitchen supplies

properties.name: Frenchie
properties.note: One menu, prix fixe, the chef is the only one who cooks. The chef trained at Gramercy Tavern in NYC

properties.name: Hôtel Amour

properties.name: Hôtel Particulier Montmartre

properties.name: Hugo Desnoyer

properties.name: L’Avant Comptoir de la Terre

properties.name: La Tour Montlhéry - Chez Denise

They do a bistro menu during the day with the best ouef mayonnaise (deviled eggs) on earth.

properties.name: Le Dôme Café

properties.name: Le Jeu de Quilles
properties.note: Tony had breakfast here with Eric Ripert

properties.name: Le Papillon
properties.note: Tony, Eric Ripert, and Joel Robouchon ate oysters at this cafe

properties.name: Le Paul Bert

properties.name: Le Pure Café
properties.note: Coffee

properties.name: Le Royal

properties.name: Le Verre Volé

properties.name: Le Wepler

properties.name: Les Cocottes

properties.name: Marché des Enfants Rouges

properties.name: Pâtisserie Pinaud Pascal

properties.name: Restaurant Au Passage

properties.name: Robert et Louise

properties.name: Rue Montorgueil

properties.name: Rue Mouffetard

properties.name: Urfa Durum
properties.note: Kurdish Sandwich shop; rolled flatbread sandwiches with grilled meats

properties.name: Vadorin Maurice
properties.note: Meat shop selling offal.

properties.name: Marché des Lices
properties.note: Tony shops the farmers' market with chef Olivier Roellinger

properties.name: Chez Jacky
properties.note: Tony finally got his huge seafood tower!

properties.name: Le Ty Billig
properties.note: Ham, egg, and cheese crepes

properties.name: Château Richeux
properties.note: Chef Olivier Roellinger's restaurant

properties.name: Ostalapia
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Miray Girt, who was born in Spanish Basque country but raised in France.They discussed the differences between French and Spanish Basque foods. They had "ventrèche de thon, or tuna belly with tomato carpaccio; pigeon, roasted and then flambéed, served with a terrine or pâté of the same bird."

properties.name: L'Espérance
properties.note: Former site of restaurant L'Espérance

properties.name: Saint-Pierre-de-Chandieu
properties.note: Tony visited Chef Boulud's childhood school in this town, where Chef Boulud explained the fresh, healthy meals students are given here. They had pumpkin soup, fish with couscous, cheese with chocolate and orange segments.

properties.name: La Cave aux Fromages
properties.note: Cheese shop

properties.name: The Little Duke
properties.note: Tony tried making pastries here

properties.name: Boucherie Charcuterie Volailles
properties.note: Butcher breaking down meats the old school way

properties.name: Hôtel du Buet
properties.note: Time for more cheese. Tony and Eric had raclette and fondue here.

properties.name: MANGO Tahiti
properties.note: The restaurant was not yet open yet when Tony visited, but the chef cooked for him

properties.name: Marché de Papeete
properties.note: Produce and meat

properties.name: Patachou
properties.note: Cafe. Coffee and baguette or croissant.

properties.name: Clouds Bar & Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and Zamir met up at this bar on the top floor of the Radisson to drink chacha, a traditional Georgian moonshine

properties.name: Dilber Gentlemen's Club
properties.note: Tony and Zamir went to this strip club after they lost money at gambling.

properties.name: Peace Casino
properties.note: Tony and Zamir gambled (and lost) at this casino. They played blackjack and roulette.

properties.name: Sazandari
properties.note: "One of the city's older, more traditional joints" Tony said. Tony and Zamir are both hungover from the previous evening's shenanigans. They had a hangover cure, khashi which Zamir explained is made of beef broth from bones and joints. Tony did not think it worked. He did enjoy the chashushuli, "a stew of slow-cooked veal with onion and tomato, heavily seasoned with coriander, fennel, garlic, and chilies."

properties.name: Pheasant's Tears Winery
properties.note: The chef, Gia Rokashvili, uses local ingredients and serves the food with local wines. Tony had boiled beets in wild plum sauce, freshly foraged mushrooms with chili pepper, mint, and tarragon; baby lamb stewed with cumin, cooked with wild rice; fresh tonis puri, bread made in a tandoor-like clay oven. He ate with Keti Bochorishvili, former Deputy Minister of Economy and Sustainable Development, and Tamuna Gvaberidze, gallery owner.

properties.name: Patara Khurvaleti
properties.note: Tony headed out to this border agrarian town of only 9 families, to have a traditional supra. A supra is a Georgian feast. Tony ate with journalist Dima Bit-Suleiman, and Ushangi & Makvala Kokashvili. They had three varieties of khachapuri, a traditional cheese-filled bread, stuffed here with potato, beet leaves, or stewed cabbage; korma made from a roasted pig; homemade bread.

properties.name: Culinarium (moved)
properties.note: Tony ate here with chef Tekuna Gachechiladze, who is trying to modernize Georgian cuisine. I have also mapped the current location of this restaurant.

properties.name: Culinarium Khasheria
properties.note: New site of chef Tekuna Gachechiladze's restaurant.

properties.name: Gabriadze
properties.note: Tony ate here with Paul Rimple, Tbilisi bureau chief for Culinary Backstreets. They had "salad with orange, almonds, and honey; grilled lamb ribs with pomegranate sauce; slices of fried eggplant wrapped around a walnut filling; chkmeruli, chicken slow-baked in an oven and then simmered in garlic and milk."

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal prepared by Meriko Gubeladze, a star chef at Tbilisi’s Black Lion Restaurant. They had spinach with walnuts and wild capers; pickled carrots; cheese curds with roasted hazelnuts and mint; roasted eggplant seasoned with coriander; kharcho, a stewed chicken cooked slow with ajika chili sauce, onions, marigold flower powder, and blue fenugreek, and cooked & served in a pumpkin.

properties.name: Sofia Melnikovas Fantastiuri Duqani
properties.note: Tony had a meal with activist and journalist Tamara Chergoleishvili, founding director of Georgia’s Tabula Media. They had khinkali which Tony describes as “big, fat, juicy soup dumplings filled with spicy cumin-jacked minced beef in a hot, rich, potentially scalding broth”.

properties.name: Bellman Bar
properties.note: Former site of Bellman Bar? Tony met with film director and photographer Miron Zownir for cocktails here.

properties.name: Domke Nino Fleischerei
properties.note: Tony met with Frank Künster, legendary Berlin bouncer. They had pork schnitzel with brown gravy and boulette (pork meatballs). They discussed the nightclub scene in Berlin.

properties.name: Fleamarket at Mauerpark
properties.note: Tony visited this flea market with Anton and his son Wolfgang. They purchased records at one stand.

properties.name: Grosz
properties.note: This address was supposed to be a place called Grosz where Tony ate Weimar fare with Historian Brendan Nash; performance artist Le Pustra; and event manager, producer, and promoter Else Edelstahl. Could not find on map.

properties.name: Hasir
properties.note: Doner kebab in a pita.

properties.name: Lichtblick-Kino
properties.note: Tony met with Billy Wagner, co-owner of Nobelhart & Schmutzig at this movie house for currywurst and fries, doner kebab, sparkling wine, and red wine.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal at Anton Newcombe's studio (frontman of the Brian Jonestown Massacre) and friends. The had roasted lamb, sweet potatoes, and homemade schnapps.

properties.name: Michelberger Hotel
properties.note: Tony ate at the restaurant in this hotel with Ellen Allien, famous Berlin DJ. They had sausage made from chicken, pig’s feet, and sage with smoked potatoes and apple; braised beef shoulder served with root vegetables, potatoes, horseradish, and kale.

properties.name: Rogacki
properties.note: Traditional German food

properties.name: Zum Schusterjungen
properties.note: Tony and rocker Anton Newcombe had a dinner of brined pork knuckle served alongside sauerkraut and boiled potatoes, schnitzel with sautéed seasonal vegetables and creamed mushrooms. And a large beer.

properties.name: Zur Currywurst
properties.note: Currywurst and fries. It's a very Berlin thing.

properties.name: Artistanbul Restaurant
properties.note: Tony met with Melek Yaprak, aTurkish-German whose grandparents were among the first wave of Turkish immigrants in Cologne in this Turkish restaurant. They had Raki and mezze of "spicy mashed vegetables, tzatziki, hummus, beetroot and olive dip, fried eggplant, pastries with feta, meatballs with tomato sauce and mint"

properties.name: Bar Trattoria Celentano
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Maurizio Arca, a Sardinian-German. They had wine, antipasti (carpaccio, tomatoes with pepper and parmesan, grilled zucchini, mortadella), spaghetti with white clam sauce

properties.name: Bei Oma Kleinmann
properties.note: Tony met with Tracey Gudwin (friend and former producer for No Reservations) and Anke Lönne, a resident of Cologne. They had schnitzel, which Bourdain describes as “surfboard-sized slabs of veal and pork filled with many wonderful things, dredged in breadcrumbs and fried in magical, magical deep fat.”

properties.name: Brauerei zur Malzmühle
properties.note: Tony met with Heinz Grüne, beer expert, to have some kolsch, a style of pale ale created in Cologne. They ate a halve hahn, "Gouda cheese on rye" and Mettbrötchen, or raw minced pork on a roll. They also had himmel und erde (blood sausage, fried onions, mashed potatoes, applesauce), and cured pork

properties.name: De Fressbud
properties.note: Tony met with the chef from Ox & Klee, Daniel Gottschlich and his friend Demetri to have grilled bratwurst with curry sauce.

properties.name: Habibi Köln
properties.note: Tony met with Syrian immigrant Sakher Al Mohamad to talk about the unrest Cologne faced when middle eastern terrorists attacked Cologne residents on New Year's Eve 2015.

properties.name: Kleine Glocke
properties.note: "A hangout for Cologne's artist community since the first World War." Tony ate sauerbraten (pot roast of horse meat) with chef René Stessl

properties.name: Ox & Klee
properties.note: Tony met with former rock star Irmin Schmidt of the Krautrock band Can. They had grilled scalllop with black salsify served with a consomme of burned hay, lemon gelee, and walnut oil; grilled lobster with a dashi of eel, marinated algae, fermented kale, and yuzu froth; braised ox cheeks with marrow, beet roots with mustard, gratin potatoes, black truffle.

properties.name: Uerige
properties.note: Serving beer and pork products since 1862. Tony met with Heinz and his friend Judith drank alt (a dark beer) and ate pickled eggs, cabbage and mettwurst, and spicy pork goulash with rye bread.

properties.name: Akosnachi Enterprise Gh.
properties.note: Tony tries street foods like fried bananas

properties.name: ASANKA LOCAL RESTAURANT
properties.note: Omo Tuo: (pounded rice balls served with palm or groundnut soup.)

properties.name: Osu Night Market
properties.note: Open 5pm to 2am. Food market selling many different foods. Tony's guide buys different ingredients from the street food vendors and assembles a plate with roast pork, spinach, beans, and rice.

properties.name: Bougatsa Iordanis
properties.note: Handmade pastry traditionally eaten for breakfast. It's a phyllo dough filled with a kind of salty ricotta style cheese

properties.name: Pantheon
properties.note: Authentic Greek food. Tripe soup, chickpeas

properties.name: Bluefin divers Naxos Diving Center-Padi 5 Star Resort
properties.note: Tony took a diving tour to see a sunken ship that sunk in 1982.

properties.name: Kalimnos
properties.note: Tony ate at a restaurant called Elitsa Rini. Could not find it so I guess it closed

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate with a family on a local farm, including the Aegan governor. They had stuffed peppers, slow-cooked rooster, rabbit in a wine sauce, and potatoes.

properties.name: Souvlucky
properties.note: Tony had lunch with local fisherman and discussed the economy. They had souvlaki, fries, and other Mediterranean goodies, and Mythos beer.

properties.name: Taverna Platanos
properties.note: Naxos style pork called rosto in a small village in the mountains.

properties.name: The Ocean Club
properties.note: Not sure if this is the beach bar where Tony had some raki to start the day. It was a ocean side bar near where the boat dropped them off.

properties.name: Μαρίνα Νάξου / Naxos Dock
properties.note: Tony had a meal on a boat with some fishermen. They had octopus, grilled salatouri (skate), marinated anchovies, grilled rofos (grouper), and mashed fava beans.

properties.name: Fish Market
properties.note: Fish market

properties.name: Neoria Fish Tavern
properties.note: Grilled octopus, sea urchin, fried cuttlefish, sheep milk cheese

properties.name: Assiette Creole
properties.note: In the relatively affluent area of Haiti, which was less damaged by the earthquake, Tony ate Creole food with some locals.

properties.name: Avenue Maïs Gaté
properties.note: This area on the edge of Port-au-Prince has a market with a lot of street food available. Laloo (a leafy green veggie with bits of crab, okra, and peppers), rice & beans, pork & chicken in Creole sauce with picklese (a spicy slaw).

properties.name: Bo Innovation
properties.note: Alvin Leung's famous and very strange modern cuisine. Tony called him the "Ferren Adria" of Hong Kong

properties.name: China Cafe
properties.note: Tony and photojournalist Simon Go ordered Fried pork cutlet over rice with tomato sauce, soup with macaroni, spam and egg, French toast with peanut butter and condensed milk, pineapple bun.

properties.name: Fishing Village Gourmet Food
properties.note: Christopher Doyle and filmmaker Jenny Suen accompanied Tony to eat corn–and–fish ball soup; fried fish ball with dried scallop, dried shrimp, and pork; sun cured salted salted egg yolks served over rice; crispy pork belly cooked in shrimp paste and yu choy

properties.name: Happy Paradise
properties.note: Tony had dinner with Jenny Suen, feng shui master Thierry Chow, and chef May Chow. They had sourdough chicken-fat egg waffle with Taiwanese bottarga; sauteed prawns with pan-roasted pumpkin, dried shrimp roe, and prawn oil; tea-smoked pigeon; Hakka-style chicken; and pig brain with burnt-pear vinaigrette.

properties.name: Keung Kee Dai Pai Dong
properties.note: "Cheap, delicious food served from open air stalls." Tony met with chef Gazza Cheng and had drunken chicken, fish tripe, and egg-custard clay-pot dish with youtiao

properties.name: Lau Sum Kee Noodle
properties.note: Tony had lunch with Douglas Young, founder of Goods of Desire. They had prawn-roe noodles.

properties.name: Leaf Dessert
properties.note: A 100-year old noodle shop. Tony had beef brisket noodles with wontons, cinematographer Chris Doyle had choy noodle soup.

properties.name: Lin Heung Tea House
properties.note: Dim sum!

properties.name: Muscle Man
properties.note: 3 bodybuilder brothers make noodles at this restaurant at night

properties.name: Ping Kee Noodles
properties.note: They ate soup at a restaurant where noodles are made by hand

properties.name: Sher-E-Punjab
properties.note: Tony had northern Indian food here.

properties.name: Sing Heung Yuen
properties.note: Weird macaroni tomato egg dish; silk stocking tea

properties.name: Sun Hing Restaurant
properties.note: Tony went for dim sum with Janice Lau and Jason Cheung, members of Hong Kong punk band David Boring.

properties.name: Tai Po Cooked Food Centre & Podium Garden
properties.note: Food stand market

properties.name: Temple Street Night Market
properties.note: Lots of food stands. Tony met with a local food blogger Josh Suh and ate claypot rice

properties.name: Tsui Wah Restaurant
properties.note: Diner style. More comfort foods

properties.name: Tung Po Kitchen
properties.note: Eat everything, but do not miss the black squid ink pasta. And DO make a reservation.

properties.name: Under Bridge Spicy Crab
properties.note: The restaurant where the chef made the chili oil typhoon crab

properties.name: Victoria Harbour
properties.note: Tony ate typhoon crab on a boat

properties.name: Yat Lok Roast Goose
properties.note: Roast goose and pig. Tony said the goose was "worth flying to Hong Kong for"

properties.name: Belvárosi Disznótoros
properties.note: Tony tried the blood sausage with braised red cabbage and pickles.

properties.name: Bufe Falatozo
properties.note: "A smoky, chilly working class joint," Tony describes of this restaurant. "A golden pancake, heaped with chicken livers, and covered, nay, drowned, with a rich, deeply satisfying sauce with bone marrow." He also tried the venison stew and the HUGE schnitzel this place is famous for.

properties.name: Gellért Thermal Bath
properties.note: A huge and beautiful thermal bathhouse Tony went to with Vilmos Zsigmond, a cinematographer who worked on movies like Close Encounters of the Third Kind. (He died in 2016.)

properties.name: New York Café
properties.note: Over 125 years old, it was the nicest cafe in the world at the time it was built where artists and writers gathered. Nowadays, they have lots of foie gras on the menu. Tony had the pork throttle stew.

properties.name: Vígszínház Street
properties.note: Tony saw a gypsy band called Budapest Bar play and then ate with the musicians. They had chicken paprikash, whole perch roasted in bacon, stuffed cabbage filled with goose meat and slow-cooked, goulash.

properties.name: Old Kőrössy Fish Restaurant
properties.note: Tony went to a restaurant from Vilmos' childhood, where they ate this fish chowder called halászlé made from pike from the river and seasoned with paprika and other spices.

properties.name: Bæjarins Beztu Pylsur
properties.note: Hot dogs

visits.title: Iceland: Hello Darkness My Old Friend

properties.name: VOX Brasserie & Bar
properties.note: Fancy schmancy

visits.title: Iceland: Hello Darkness My Old Friend

properties.name: Þrír frakkar
properties.note: Icelandic food

visits.title: Iceland: Hello Darkness My Old Friend

properties.name: Toddy Shop
properties.note: Toddy is an alcoholic drink, but Tony was not a fan. He was a fan of the food served here. Known for their fish head curry. He also tried steamed pearl spot fish curry (local fish), and crab masala.

properties.name: All India Famous Amritsari Kulcha
properties.note: Iconic dish of Punjab. "A perfect little flavor bomb of wheat dough pressed against the side of a very, very hot clay oven, slathered with butter and served with a chickpea curry on the side."

properties.name: Beera Chicken House
properties.note: Tony had tandoori chicken and fresh baked naan

properties.name: Golden Temple
properties.note: Tony called this the "Sikh equivalent of the Vatican." He attended a festival called a gurpurab where feet are cleansed and people of all religions all take part in a simple meal

properties.name: Pritam Dhaba
properties.note: "Get used to eating a lot of vegetarian. India is one of the few places on Earth where, even for me, that's not a burden," Tony says. He had chickpeas, saag, and lots of naan.

properties.name: STEEL HOUSE
properties.note: Tony ate at a street food stand outside this building. Fish, quail, lentils.

properties.name: Street Foods
properties.note: Tony ate street foods

properties.name: Marudhar Restaurant
properties.note: Outdoor vegetarian restaurant

properties.name: Chapslee
properties.note: Tony stayed at this place where a maharaja once lived. He ate some of the food such as mulligatawny soup.

properties.name: Shimla
properties.note: "Here the colonials created England in miniature," Tony says of this hill town.

properties.name: Tea Stall And Snacks Stall
properties.note: Tony had some snacks at this train station on his way to the Himalayas.

properties.name: Natraj Restaurant Udaipur (Dining Hall/Rajasthani Thali/Gujarati Thali)
properties.note: All you can eat for less than a dollar. Thali style vegetarian food.

properties.name: Taj Lake Palace
properties.note: Tony had a martini at this fancy hotel on a lake

properties.name: Penida Island
properties.note: Tony took a boat ride to this island and had fresh lobster with an anthropologist on this island that used to be a penal colony.

properties.name: Carano Masakan Padang
properties.note: Closed now, this was a restaurant called Carano Masakan Padang. Tony had a lunch here, served similar to tapas style with lots of rice. Beef rendang, stews. His cameraman knocked over a shelf holding all of the food for the day for the entire restaurant.

properties.name: Kamandalu Ubud
properties.note: Not sure if this is the hotel where Tony sat at the pool and said “So this, too, is Bali, I guess — or it is now. Thank you, Jimmy Buffett, for taking a big dump on the world. Another wagyu slider, my good man. Yes, thank you. By weight, how much human waste is generated by your average person on vacation. Look around you. Do the math. I want a water sample. I’m telling you, the fecal coliform count is going to be interesting. I’m sure there’s a metaphor here. Am I bitter? Am I just old? Do I just have, like, a predisposition of instinctive hatred of young people? Perhaps. Enjoy the moment.…

properties.name: Rumah Makan SURYA Masakan Padang
properties.note: Tony ate Padang food: fried lungs, intestine goulash, beef rendang.

properties.name: Blok M Square
properties.note: Tony had street food at this huge market

properties.name: Ibu Oka
properties.note: Tony had roasted pig here.

properties.name: Abbasi Great Mosque
properties.note: The second largest public square in the world

properties.name: Bethlehem Church
properties.note: Tony visited this church in the Isfahan province

properties.name: Haaj Mahmoud Shefa'at Beryani
properties.note: Tony had Iranian biryani here.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had more Persian food in someone's home. They ate Boz Ghormeh (slow cooked lamb in yogurt, saffron, and egg yolks), sour cherry rice, koofteh tabrizi (a very large meatball with ground beef, onion, cooked rice, walnuts, dried apricots, boiled eggs, and barberries).

properties.name: (Tabbakhi Neshat) طباخی نشاط‎‎
properties.note: Tony had sheephead stew and bread at this cafe.

properties.name: DiziBar Persian Cuisine
properties.note: Tony had a dish called Dizi, named after the vessel it is cooked in

properties.name: Milad Tower
properties.note: A symbol of national pride, the tallest building. Tony visited the observation deck. During the shoot, a huge sandstorm rolled through the area, causing parts of the building to fly off. This was all captured on film.

properties.name: Tehran
properties.note: The best food in Iran is made at people's houses. Tony's host cooked him a huge meal in her house. They had chicken cream soup, Fesenjan (a stew of fried chicken, onion, ground walnuts, pomegranate, dried apricot, tomato paste), Mahia Halva (tilapia), Ghemieh (beans and meat), Tahdig (crispy rice).

properties.name: Abu Shahab City Restaurant
properties.note: Tony ate with soldiers from the US Army who were stationed there. Tony had kebab.

properties.name: Qardaran Tea Shop
properties.note: The market near the citadel is "a dizzying maze of alleys and vendors" where you can buy everything from guns to food. Tony had tea at this shop

properties.name: The Ivory Tower
