properties.note: Stew with lots of offal

properties.name: Celtic Whiskey Shop & Wines on the Green
properties.note: Rare whiskeys

properties.name: Chapter One Restaurant
properties.note: Traditional fine dining (michelin star)

properties.name: Coppinger Row
properties.note: Great cocktails

properties.name: CrackBird South William
properties.note: Fried chicken

properties.name: Fallon & Byrne, Food Hall
properties.note: Market and eateries

properties.name: Four Seasons Hotel Dublin
properties.note: Where Tony stayed

properties.name: John Kavanagh
properties.note: Drink a Guinness here. Locals call this the Gravediggers Pub since its next to the cemetery. Great food.

properties.name: King Sitric Restaurant & Accommodation
properties.note: Fresh seafood, lots of shellfish

properties.name: Kinlay House Dublin
properties.note: A hostel

properties.name: Lolly and Cooks
properties.note: Sausage rolls

properties.name: Madina Desi Curry Co
properties.note: Chickpea curry

properties.name: Matt The Thresher
properties.note: Oysters

properties.name: Mulligan's
properties.note: Pub

properties.name: O'Donovans Newsagents
properties.note: Handheld version of the Irish breakfast served here

properties.name: Roma
properties.note: Fish n chips. With curry sauce. The smoked cod is good

properties.name: Slattery's
properties.note: Traditional Irish breakfast

properties.name: The Cake Cafe
properties.note: Breakfast dishes, crepes, cakes

properties.name: The Chophouse
properties.note: Gastropub

properties.name: The Cobblestone
properties.note: Irish music

properties.name: The Davenport Hotel
properties.note: Reasonable rates

properties.name: The Forty Foot
properties.note: People dive off the cliffs here

properties.name: The Palace Bar
properties.note: Over 100 whiskeys

properties.name: The Whiskey Bar
properties.note: Great selection of whiskeys

properties.name: Ulysses Rare Books Ltd
properties.note: Used and rare books. The name changed since the show. Formerly known as Cathach Books.

properties.name: Majda
properties.note: "It feels like an alternate universe..." Tony says of this restaurant run by a couple, where the husband is Muslim and the wife is Jewish. He ate fried eggs with peppers from the garden; roasted tomatoes, okra, onions, and mint; grilled zucchini covered in fried yogurt sauce; fresh zucchini with mint and tiny sweet apricots

properties.name: מידס‎
properties.note: Tony spoke with the owner of this restaurant whose daughter was killed. They discussed both sides of the conflict.

properties.name: Damascus Gate
properties.note: Writer Yotam Ottolenghi took Tony to East Jerusalem, where Palestinians live. They had falafel from a street vendor

properties.name: Western Wailling Wall
properties.note: Tony is not a religious person, half Jewish and half Catholic, he felt uncomfortable here at the Wall.

properties.name: Palazzo Margherita Hotel
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Francis Ford Coppola. They had lampascioni, or scored and fried hyacinth bulbs; braciole di cotenna, or stuffed pig skin rolls; lamb’s head casserole, fresh fruit, assorted wines.

properties.name: Il Calice Wine Bar and Food Bologna
properties.note: Tony shared a Negroni and some thin sliced mortadella with fresh bread with Chef Michael White

properties.name: Quadrilatero
properties.note: Tony met with Chef Michael White in this food market

properties.name: Trattoria Fita
properties.note: Tony and Michael had housemade proscuitto, ficattola (fried bread), a local cheese, tortelli, gnocchi, and steak

properties.name: Al Lardo Al Lardo
properties.note: Cured fat of the pig made in the Apennine mountains. Where Dante came to write.

properties.name: Catania Market
properties.note: Very old market selling meats, produce, cheese, and other local goods. The market is best known for its seafood.

properties.name: D'Antone
properties.note: Tony tried horse meat here. They grilled it outside on the street. Tony enjoyed it.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had dinner at Tomaso's mother's home. They had caponata; bread and tomato salad; shrimp sautéed in garlic, butter, and herbs; sardines fillet sautéed in garlic, oil, and red pepper

properties.name: Pappacarbone
properties.note: Rocco made lunch for Tony at his restaurant. A dish with 2 types of clams and pasta; octopus in a red sauce

properties.name: Parco dei Nebrodi
properties.note: Tony went to a free range pig farm located in this national park. They cooked one of them, using the lungs and liver in a stew with garlic, chili pepper, red wine. They also make blood sausage and serve it with homemade cured meats, local cheese, and homemade wine.

properties.name: Ricciolandia
properties.note: Tony had uni pasta and beer.

properties.name: Pronto Pesce
properties.note: Another Bacari with a more modern feel. Tony enjoyed the sardines gratine.

properties.name: Fattoria Casaloste
properties.note: Wine and cheese

properties.name: Old Butcher Cecchini
properties.note: He quotes "Heat" by Bill Buford, who calls this place "a museum of Tuscan cooking"

properties.name: Formaggeria salumeria gastronomia Cavallotti
properties.note: Local cheese shop. Tony's chef friend made uvo raviolo.

properties.name: San Domenico
properties.note: Michelin starred restaurant. Seared tuna with borlotti bean puree; turbot confit with fresh asparagus; clams; spaghetti di gragnano with mussels, calamari, sea urchin, bottarga, shrimp; gnocchi with lardo, fresh fava beans, black truffle. Tableside prepared roast duck.

properties.name: Tre Monti Azienda Agricola srl
properties.note: Vineyard. The chef in the vineyard's kitchen made tagliatelle. They ate it outside with a Sangiovese made at the vineyard.

properties.name: L'Angolo degli amici
properties.note: Former site of this restaurant, which translated to Friends' Corner.

properties.name: Uccellina
properties.note: Family owned traditional Italian. Known for their wild boar.

properties.name: Matera
properties.note: A cave city that predates the Greek and Roman empires. Tony visited the city with Asia Argento. It used to be where the outcasts were sent, until Hollywood came to make movies. Now there are tourists and AirBnBs.

properties.name: Masseria Spina Resort
properties.note: Tony had a pasta dinner here with Asia Argento.

properties.name: Pizzeria Pellone
properties.note: Margherita pizza

properties.name: Al Convento
properties.note: A convent converted into a restaurant, only ingredients that are fresh and currently in season are served

properties.name: Farmhouse Maison Rosset
properties.note: Tony hopped the border to Italy because he craved pasta in the Aoeste Valley. They had gnocchi with beet root fondue, ravioli with tomato sauce, creamy polenta with braised beef. Tony, Eric, and host played feur lane, a game where "you try to hit that tiny polished stone with that stick as far as you can."

properties.name: Antica Focacceria San Francesco
properties.note: One of Italy's oldest restaurants. Pizza, sandwiches, bread.

properties.name: Ballaro Market
properties.note: Open air food market

properties.name: Frittola
properties.note: Fried meat fritters

properties.name: Trattoria Piccolo Napoli
properties.note: Tony met with food writer Mary Taylor Simeti, food writer, for lunch. They had panelli (fritter made from chickpeas), caponata (sweet and sour eggplant), spaghetti al nero di seppia (spaghetti in cuttlefish ink), casarecce al pescespada (pasta with swordfish, eggplant, and tomatoes)

properties.name: Bar Caffetteria
properties.note: Tony had espresso and a bomba, a type of Italian donut, for breakfast.

properties.name: Belvedere Cederna
properties.note: Open area with view of Colosseum

properties.name: Betto e Mary
properties.note: Quintessential Roman food

properties.name: Cacio e Pepe
properties.note: Pasta

properties.name: Cafe Faggiani
properties.note: Cappuccinos (only in the morning) and cornetto

properties.name: Cafe' De Paris
properties.note: Seasonal restaurant; Tony had a dish that had fresh artichokes, fava, peas, and pancetta. Also a baby lamb dish, a salad with roman chicory and anchovy.

properties.name: Freni e Frizoni
properties.note: Cocktails and snacks

properties.name: Gelateria dei Gracchi
properties.note: Gelato

properties.name: Gladiators
properties.note: Tony and Asia Argento had pasta for dinner ringside at a boxing match.

properties.name: Hotel Majestic
properties.note: Tony ate at the Sicilian restaurant in the hotel. Caponata, bucatini with sardines. Fish, potatoes, fava, pistachio, tomato.

properties.name: I Porchettoni
properties.note: PORK

properties.name: La Grattachecca
properties.note: Italian Ice

properties.name: Osteria dal 1931
properties.note: Tony ate by himself here, he had Chianti, prosciutto and artichoke hearts in olive oil, ravioli.

properties.name: Palazzo dei Congressi
properties.note: Tony and Asia visited this huge structure, which was built originally to be a symbol of fascism.

properties.name: Pascucci
properties.note: Fresh fruit smoothies

properties.name: Pesce Fritto E Baccala'
properties.note: Fried fish and fried zucchini

properties.name: Pizzarium
properties.note: Pizza

properties.name: Roma Sparita
properties.note: Tony had Cacio e Pepe here

properties.name: Roscioli Salumeria con Cucina
properties.note: Tony and his friend went to the back of the shop to try fresh buffalo mozzarella and prosciutto

properties.name: Salumeria Volpetti
properties.note: Cured meats, cheeses, breads for a picnic in a piazza.

properties.name: Simply Market
properties.note: Tony and Asia went shopping here for a lunch her sister prepared for them. They bought fava beans, cheese, and other goodies.

properties.name: Trattoria A Casa Di Rita
properties.note: Tony and his wife Ottavia ate here with one of the cameraman. Antipasti; tagliolini with garlic, chiles, and pork ragout; oxtail; tripe.

properties.name: Trattoria Il Timoniere Roma
properties.note: Tony ate carbonara and amatriciana

properties.name: Trattoria Morgana
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Abel Ferrara, film director known for Kings of New York and Bad Lieutenant, who now lives in Rome with his family. They had Roman pastas, snails, and artichokes.

properties.name: Trattoria Settimio
properties.note: The restaurant that Tony and Asia visited in Rome that Tony refused to disclose the name of.

properties.name: Trattoria Sora Lella
properties.note: Old Italian Classics

properties.name: Zia Forica
properties.note: Octopus, anchovy, cordula, fava beans, snails

properties.name: Osteria Nero d'Avola
properties.note: Tony had dinner here after the disastrous "fishing" expedition. It's his birthday and he doesn't remember any of the meal because he drank so much. Tomaso and his wife (locals) had the meal together. They ate white olives, bread, olive oil, abalone served raw in the shell, baby sardines called neonata with citrus and salt, octopus, tuna tartare, cuttlefish crudo, pasta alfino querta (a true Sicilian classic made with fennel, pine nuts, saffron, and anchovy. Served with anchovy curing in chestnuts and salt for days, then lightly fried in oil.

properties.name: Spiaggia libera di Isola Bella
properties.note: The beach where they launched the boat to go "fishing" and then dropped dead fish, octopus, and shellfish into the water around Tony. He described his feelings on this experience saying he slid "quickly into a spiral of near hysterical depression." After this scene he gets really drunk.

properties.name: Al Covo
properties.note: The chef made tiny soft shell crabs caught that day

properties.name: Antica Locanda Montin
properties.note: Simple classics done well.

properties.name: Cantina Do Mori
properties.note: Cicchetto - sort of like tapas or pinchos. A bar that serves these is called a Bacari.

properties.name: Mercato di Rialto
properties.note: Fresh fish market

properties.name: Osteria Al Ponte del Diavolo
properties.note: German-style food and wines. This was his guide's favorite place

properties.name: Trattoria da Romano
properties.note: Open since the 1800s. Guestbooks have sketches from famous artists like Matisse. Simple, classic, traditional Italian foods. Cooked over a wood fire and lots of seafood. The seafood risotto is spectacular

properties.name: Trattoria San Basilio
properties.note: After visiting on a tugboat pulling large ships into dock, they had a meal at this trattoria on an outside patio table. Tony called it "Trattoria Borghi" so it may have changed ownership

properties.name: Monaci delle Terre Nere
properties.note: Agri tourismo - a working farm, hotel, restaurant. Produces olive oil. On his last day in Sicily, he had a meal here made from ingredients on the farm: penne with sundried tomatoes and zucchini; a rabbit dish; agridolce greens

properties.name: GoldenEye
properties.note: Ian Fleming's cottage (British author known for creating the James Bond character). It is now a resort.

properties.name: Cynthia’s on Winifred
properties.note: At this public beach, this food stand sells ackee, banana, and salt cod.

properties.name: Piggy's Jerk Centre
properties.note: Tony ate at a jerk stand called Piggy's in this town. Unfortunately it burned down in 2019

properties.name: Trident Hotel
properties.note: Tony was the only guest in this "expensive, luxurious" hotel. Tony had dinner with the owner of the hotel, one of the richest men in the world, local billionaire Michael Lee Chin.

properties.name: Aunt Merl Fish And Lobster Restaurant
properties.note: Seafood Restaurant on the beach. Fried, Steamed, Brown Stew, or Festival Style.

properties.name: Chris’s Cook Shop Main Street
properties.note: Tony had oxtail, curried goat, callaloo, rice and pigeon peas.

properties.name: Dr. Hoe Rum Bar
properties.note: Tony had a traditional fisherman’s breakfast, and a Steel Bottom (one part rum, four parts beer).

properties.name: Ryokan Sekiyou
properties.note: A getaway for affluent Japanese.

properties.name: Rakuichi Soba
properties.note: The chef, Master Tatsui Rai, makes the noodles by hand.

properties.name: Yakitori Torimatsu
properties.note: The master Akihiro Matsuosa grills meats

properties.name: Yamanaka Onsen
properties.note: Tony described this area as "unlike anyplace I've ever been in Japan." Masa's good friend and pottery mentor, Haruo Konishi has a family hideaway here. They made iwana (char) caught that day in a mountain stream, Hokkaido scallops, wild Japanese boar, grilled fukinoto, and soba noodles with duck, green onion, and dashi sauce.

properties.name: Higashi Chaya District
properties.note: Tony and Masa watched a geiko perform at Fujinoya Tea House (could not find exact location on map).

properties.name: Hosho Sushi
properties.note: Tony attended a meet and greet here for Cook it Raw elite chefs like Rene Redzepi, Albert Adria, Claude Bosi, etc.

properties.name: Ōmichō Market
properties.note: Tony and Masa ate fresh seafood at this market. They had sea urchin, kanogani (snow crab), grilled eel livers, and giant oysters "as big as freakin' clown shoes"

properties.name: Yamanoo
properties.note: Traditional kaiseki restaurant that dates back to 1890. Menu changes as seasonal ingredients become available. ""Preserved in a tightly wrapped bamboo leaf, intended to resemble a sword, sea bream served chimaki-style over rice slightly sweetened by vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, and sesame... Grilled rockfish made Horaku style, steamed in smoking wormwood. Bamboo shoots and wagyu beef prepared tableside with soy sauce and mirin. Today's sashimi course is cod, salted and pressed between leaves of kelp for 2 hours, coated with sake-cured codfish roe. Next to it, flounder, its skin gently rubbed …

properties.name: Hiiragiya
properties.note: Ryokan (traditional Japanese inn). Tony had a kaiseki meal with Chef Morimoto

properties.name: Lawson Kokusai Street kumoji
properties.note: A mini market. Tony is obsessed with the egg salad sandwich. In the behind the scenes episode in season 12, the crew explained that the location they shot at ran out of egg salad sandwiches and there was a mad rush to find one at nearby Lawson stores.

properties.name: Makishi Market
properties.note: A public food market where you can pick out the fish and they will cook it for you. Tony had snapper and parrot fish sashimi; sea grapes dressed in vinegar; gurukun (unofficial national fish of Okinawa) battered and deep-fried; porcupine fish, battered and deep-fried.

properties.name: The DOJO bar
properties.note: Tony had drinks, horse meat and goat sashimi, pork belly, pickled pig’s ears, baked yam

properties.name: Urizun
properties.note: Tony had pork belly (cooked with stock, heavily infused with bonito flakes), pork ears thinly sliced and dressed in rice-wine vinaigrette, slow-roasted ribs brined in sake and seasonings.

properties.name: Nasushiobara
properties.note: The rural town where Masa grew up. Tony and Masa visited his family (mom and daughter). His mother made motsuni, "a slow simmered stew of pork tripe, konnyaku, daikon, green onions, and miso," sashimi with freshly grated wasabi, uni, and otoro (tuna).

properties.name: Sugimura
properties.note: Masa's brother Kazuo had a restaurant in Nasushiobara called Sugimura. Could not find it on the map. They had "kimoyaki, eel liver dipped in a sauce of sake, soy, and mirin, and grilled low and slow over charcoal... insanely delicious custard of eel and egg, jacked with bean curd, bonito broth, and kelp... hitsumabushi, eel steamed, dressed, and grilled, over rice."

properties.name: Gettouan
properties.note: "A private home turned restaurant serving very traditional Okinawan dishes," Tony describes.Tony had "tundaabun" or multiple bite-size portions served in a lacquered dish of "sliced squid; swordfish wrapped in seaweed and simmered in stock and fermented sake; dried sea snake, slow-simmered; burdock root wrapped in pork loin, slow-cooked in hot sauce; taro flash-fried and dressed with sugar and soy; pork shoulder dredged in black sesame, then steamed."

properties.name: Gosakudon
properties.note: Baseball sports bar with sushi

properties.name: Honke hormone dojo
properties.note: Horumonyaki - grilled pork and beef organs

properties.name: Jiyuken
properties.note: Curry mixed rice

properties.name: Kani Doraku Dotombori Honten
properties.note: Crab

properties.name: Kushikatsu Daruma Dotombori
properties.note: Fried meat fritters

properties.name: Aji No Karyu
properties.note: Tony had a very untraditional miso ramen that included butter and corn.

properties.name: CENTRAL WHOLESALE SEAFOOD MARKET
properties.note: Huge seafood market with crab, fish, and more.

properties.name: Wakakoma Restaurant
properties.note: Tony craved sea urchin and salmon roe.

properties.name: Kita Onsen（株）北温泉旅館
properties.note: Tony met with Masa's high school friends and had a soak in the hot spring, then ate sukiyaki (meat slowly cooked, tableside, in a broth), Tochigi beef with maitake (type of mushroom), and home-brewed sake

properties.name: Arashio-beya Sumo Stable
properties.note: Not open to the public. Tony wanted to find out what the sumo fighters eat.

properties.name: Bar Albatros
properties.note: "Definition of a hole in the wall," says Tony.

properties.name: BAR PIANO
properties.note: A nomiya, which is a tiny bar where late shift workers go out after work

properties.name: Bar 石の華 Ishinohana
properties.note: Fancy cocktails at one of the best bars in Tokyo, in Shibuya

properties.name: Chanko Edosawa
properties.note: Big pot of soup that you add things to: shrimp, chicken, veggies. This is what sumo fighters eat.

properties.name: Daitoryo
properties.note: Tony meets with Tamika (a professional dominatrix; Kinoko Hajime, a shibari (Japanese form of rope bondage) artist and Tamika’s partner; and Naga, a translator also joined) at this Izakaya restaurant. They have Kusaia, which is grilled fermented fish, yakitori, and motsunabe (intestine stew with miso).

properties.name: Ginza Sushi Ko Honten
properties.note: One of the BEST sushi places in the world. Masa learned here. They had seared horse mackerel over green onion and ginger, maguro (bluefin tuna), Japanese tiger prawn, octopus, fluke sashimi, striped jack, anago (sea eel) rolled in seaweed, and tamagoyaki (breaded shrimp eggs and fish paste)

properties.name: HACHI
properties.note: Chef Rene Redzepi met with Tony to eat here. Spinach with codfish sperm.

properties.name: Honmura An
properties.note: Very good soba noodle. Simple noodle dish served with a duck broth, pieces of duck, and scallion.

properties.name: Ivan Ramen
properties.note: Former site of Ivan Ramen, a restaurant that has since closed. The owner opened a spot in New York by the same name.

properties.name: Lawson Ningyocho Station Shop
properties.note: I am not sure if this is the exact location where David Chang introduced Tony to this convenience store, which he later wrote about several times in his future visits to Japan. It is a chain of stores, and both David and Tony loved the egg salad sandwich and the chicken nuggets here.

properties.name: Mahakala
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Chef Yasuda, they had kushiage (fried skewers of shrimp and basil, lotus fruit, octopus, and quail eggs) and okonomiyaki (egg-batter pancake stuffed with squid). Yasuda lives near here and loves this place.

properties.name: Mienoumi
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Toshio Maeda, the manga illustrator credited with inventing tentacle porn. They ate katsuo no tataki (seared bonito with daikon, fresh greens, sprouts, and ponzu) and tomato nabe (a hot pot of meat and vegetables favored by sumo wrestlers).

properties.name: Robot Restaurant
properties.note: Tony said this was the greatest show he's ever seen, better than Jimi Hendrix, Janis Joplin, and David Bowie live. He saw the show called Robot Wars.

properties.name: Sarashina Horii Azabujūban Honten
properties.note: Tony and Chef Morimoto ate soba noodles here. They have been making soba noodles by hand here since 1789

properties.name: Toriki
properties.note: Yakitori, or Japanese BBQ, mostly chicken.

properties.name: Toshima City
properties.note: There used to be a boxing gym in Toshima called Yurakura Gym, but I could not find it on the map.

properties.name: TSUKIJI FISH MARKET
properties.note: Fresh fish market

properties.name: Unagi Hosakaya
properties.note: Tony and Chef Ivan ate this restaurant that serves grilled eel.

properties.name: XEX
properties.note: This is Chef Morimoto's flagship restaurant. He prepared monkfish many different ways for Tony.

properties.name: Yakitori Alley
properties.note: Casual Japanese barbecued meats served with beer and sake.

properties.name: Yakitori Alley, Memory Lane
properties.note: Grilled meats

properties.name: Yakitori Ton Ton
properties.note: Tony met up with David Chang again after the Cook it Raw competition and had yakitori.

properties.name: Yasuda
properties.note: Tony loves Yasuda in NYC, and wanted to find out why the chef, whom the restaurant is named for, would suddenly move back to Tokyo. Yasuda said that Japan is the origin of sushi, so he wanted to return to where it was created. Yasuda is also a karate master.

properties.name: にびき
properties.note: Nibiki Restaurant. They serve fugu here, a fish that can kill you if not cooked properly.

properties.name: 田川
properties.note: Tagawa Sushi

properties.name: Ishikawa Dome
properties.note: Tony watched bull sumo, or Tōgyū, while eating yakitori.

properties.name: Lewa Wildlife Conservancy
properties.note: Tony and Kamau visited this conservancy to view the animals. They saw a giraffe, termite mounds, zebras, an ostrich, an elephant, a rhino, and some lions. They then had a breakfast of scrambled eggs and toast with Mike Watson, CEO of the conservancy; Faith Riunga, education officer of the conservancy; Wanjiku Kinuthia, communications officer of the conservancy; Tom Lalampaa, CEO of Northern Rangelands Trust

properties.name: Unknown small restaurants
properties.note: Tony met up with W. Kamau Bell in the capital city. They had a lunch of grilled tilapia, ugali, Tusker beer. Tony also had beers with Njeri Gikera, owner of ChilliMango clothing store; Melissa Mbugua, managing partner at MNM Consulting Africa; and Bell at another local place.

properties.name: Kibera slum
properties.note: Tony and Kamau visited this area and met with comedian Mammito Eunice, musician Simon Okuku, and comedian Geoffrey Ochieng, members of Kibera Creative Arts.

properties.name: Nyeri Shuttle Matatu Stage
properties.note: Tony and Kamau rode on the Mad Max bus, which is basically a party on a bus. Afterward they ate goat's head soup and drank beer.

properties.name: Toi Main Market
properties.note: Tony and Kamau went shoe shopping at this discount market

properties.name: Il Ngwesi Community
properties.note: Tony and Kamau met with Masai chief Jonathan Kip and Masai elder Lela Kinyaga to have a meal in a Masai village. They had cow’s blood mixed with milk, grilled meat, and rice.

properties.name: Boun Ork Phansa
properties.note: Tony visited during this Buddhist event that marks the end of Buddhist lent. There is a fire lantern festival, the symbolic casting away of one's sins. Tony watched a boat race on the Mekong River.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Chef James Syhabuot and his friends/family. They had aw lahm, which is made with wood ear mushrooms, dill, pepper wood (a key ingredient infused into the dish, which imparts flavors of star anise and white pepper), two types of lahm made with buffalo (one raw, one cooked), and leuat paeng, a dish made from raw buffalo blood and herbs.

properties.name: Phosy Market
properties.note: Tony met with James Syhabout, a chef and restaurateur behind two-Michelin star restaurant Commis, in San Francisco. They had a bowl of khao piak sen, or Lao noodle soup, which is James' first dish he seeks out once he gets off the plane. The dish includes crispy sliced pork, blood cake, meat broth.

properties.name: Villa Ban Lao Hotel
properties.note: "During the Secret War, [this hotel] was the offices for USAID, America's foreign aid mission," says Tony. He had lunch here with the hotel owner, Mama Vahn, who made the meal, "an elaborate spread of dishes whose recipes are left over from imperial times." They had lon song, a fish from the Mekong River, cooked with shallots, garlic, chili, coconut milk, and pork; river crab, fish with pork grilled in banana leaves, ginger sauce, pork skin, and sticky rice.

properties.name: Phonsavang Market
properties.note: They ate soup for breakfast (Khao Pia). It has chicken, duck, and pork with Vietnamese noodles.

properties.name: UXO Lao
properties.note: Tony met with workers from this organization tasked with finding unexploded objects left behind by American forces during the Secret War and safely blowing them up. Only 0.5% of the country has been cleared of unexploded devices. Kids and adults have been maimed and dismembered by these bombs strewn about the countryside.

properties.name: Al Falamanki Sodeco
properties.note: Tony had tabbouleh, kofta with yogurt sauce and cranberries, stuffed grape leaves, kibbeh.

properties.name: Al Rawda - Shatila - Restaurant
properties.note: Tony had fish, a meze of hummus and salads, Laziza nonalcoholic beer.

properties.name: Falafel M Sayhoun Original Shop
properties.note: Two falafel shops owned by brothers who do not speak to each other, right next door to each other.

properties.name: Falafel M. Sahyoun
properties.note: Two falafel shops owned by brothers who do not speak to each other, right next door to each other.

properties.name: Frank Würst Fine Hotdog
properties.note: They had drunken BBQ and chili dogs.

properties.name: Le Chef
properties.note: Tony went back to Le Chef, where he ate on his last visit (when Beirut was bombed). They had hummus with pine nuts and ground beef, eggplant with tomatoes, chickpeas, and onions. Fun story he told on this episode: The airport got bombed so after he finally got home taking a ship back to NYC, his girlfriend Ottavia met him when the boat docked and they conceived their daughter.

properties.name: MusicHall Starco
properties.note: This huge night club has many different kinds of acts. Tony met with the owner.

properties.name: Onno Bistro - Bourj Hammoud
properties.note: Tony ate Kibbeh, phyllo, Armenian cheese, spicy potatoes, meat & cheese filled pastries, hummus, stuffed grape leaves

properties.name: Radio Beirut
properties.note: Tony met with a rapper named Chino to talk about this radio station that hosts all types of musicians.

properties.name: Rizk Chicken
properties.note: "Broasted" chicken shawarma

properties.name: Souk El Tayeb
properties.note: Bringing together different regions of artisanal and organic foods in a market. "Southern coastal bread bakers, kibbeh specialists from the mountains, wild herbs & vegetables from the Bekaa Valley, and pastries made from almost forgotten recipes. It's all here," Tony says.

properties.name: Tawlet
properties.note: A rotating cast of cooks provide regional foods from the artisanal market. Labneh, Armenian meat pie, artichoke and fava bean salad, fuul.

properties.name: Massaya Winery
properties.note: Anise seed liquor, red and white table wines. Food at the restaurant changes seasonally. Tony ate potato salad with parsley, olive oil, and nuts; Lebanese spaghetti (served with yogurt); cabbage salad; grilled eggplant, tomato, and haloumi, sheep's milk cheese, labneh.

properties.name: Duala Market
properties.note: Tony shopped here for palm nuts, hot chilis, snails

properties.name: World Trade Center
properties.note: Grilled meats. Tony and the Bishop visited after the church service

properties.name: Leptis Magna
properties.note: They stopped to get a liver sandwich at a stand on the way to this spot. Leptis Magna, "arguably the most intact remains of a Roman city in the world."

properties.name: Misrata
properties.note: Tony and crew headed to this town to film, but it was on full lockdown as they approached. They walked around the ruins of the town which showed the damage done when the rebellion took down Ghadafi. Afterward, they killed a lamb and barbecued it on the coast of the Mediterranean

properties.name: مطعم بركودا 1‎
properties.city: Tajura' wa an Nawahi al Arba
properties.note: Barakoda Restaurant, a seafood restaurant just outside of town on the Mediterranean. The menu is not printed, you select fish from the daily catch on ice and they cook it for you the way you specify. Tony ate fish, shrimp, and stuffed calamari

properties.name: Bab al-Azizia
properties.note: Former site of Gadhafi's palace, destroyed by rebels in 2011. They were told to leave while they were filming this scene.

properties.name: Fashloum
properties.note: Somewhere in this neighborhood, Tony had a breakfast of sfinz, a donut with an egg on top.

properties.name: Martyrs' Square
properties.note: Tony watched as kids skating, fireworks being set off, motorcycles drove around as people celebrated the Prophet Mohammed's birthday and freedom after 50 years of oppression.

properties.name: Radisson Blu Al Mahary Hotel
properties.note: The hotel where Tony and the crew stayed while in Tripoli.

properties.name: Tripoli
properties.note: Tony went to a men-only coffeehouse called Yahzakem Coffee where people smoked hookah and drank local coffee.

properties.name: Tripoli Libia
properties.note: Somewhere in Tripoli, Tony ate at a place called Uncle Kentucky (or Kentaki) Fried Chicken. Could not find it on any maps.

properties.name: Tai Lei Loi Kei
properties.note: Pork chop bun, a Macanese specialty. It's a pork chop on a fluffy bun.

properties.name: Chez Mariette
properties.note: Tony had a famous goose dish, served with broth made from chicken and ginger, rice, and a salad

properties.name: Look's Andravoahangy
properties.note: Tony and Darren Aronofsky visited this restaurant where Tony ate pork and Darren (a vegetarian) had veggies and beans.

properties.name: Muguet
properties.note: Tony met with Darren Aronofsky here to begin their journey

properties.name: Manakara
properties.note: Tony ate shark, lobster, shrimp, zebra, lots of veggies (Darren just had veggies) on the beach with some locals.

properties.name: Ranomafana National Park
properties.note: Tony and Darren got to see golden lemurs. There are only 500 alive in the world. Tony then watched the village people butcher a cow while Darren took photos and relaxed by a stream.

properties.name: Sahambavy train station
properties.note: Tony and Darren took a very old train to Manakara.

properties.name: Muthu Restaurant
properties.note: Banana leaf curry. Steamed rice with a variety of curry dishes and side dishes placed on banana leaf.

properties.name: New Lane Street Foodstalls
properties.note: "Street by day, food wonderland by night." Tony had Char Koay Teow, a fried rice stick with bean sprouts, chili, and prawns.

properties.name: Pasar Air Itam Laksa
properties.note: Tony always had a bowl of laksa when he visited Malaysia. This dish has rice noodles, herbs, red onion, fish broth, chili

properties.name: Red Garden Food Paradise
properties.note: A food court full of stands. Tony and guide had fish head curry.

properties.name: Restoran Nasi Kandar Line Clear
properties.note: "Lots of people, lots of food, lots of cats." A 24 hour food stand. The plate they got had fried chicken, fish roe, beef, shrimp, squid, veggies, sauces, rice.

properties.name: The Jetty Food Court
properties.note: Tony called this place CF Market. They had Pork Mee: Hokkien Chinese noodles, Penang style; Balitlong (sea snails)

properties.name: Aunty Aini's Garden Cafe
properties.note: Tony sat with the chef and ate roast beef, poached egg, chicken rendang.

properties.name: Big Tree Lin Kee chan sow lin steamed fish head
properties.note: Fish heads!

properties.name: Nasi Lemak Antarabangsa
properties.note: Street food

properties.name: Restoran Seetharam Family Curry House
properties.note: Tony ate here with a tattoo artist that did his ouroboros tattoo, Eddie David of Borneo Ink.

properties.name: Wong Ah Wah Restaurant
properties.note: After a very long plane ride, Tony had char kway teow (dish made with rice noodles), spicy pork noodles, black pepper crab, beer.

properties.name: Choon Hui Cafe 春园茶室
properties.note: Tony calls Sarawak laksa "a magical dish," it has chicken, prawn, coconut, chili, and noodles in a complex broth. “Best breakfast ever.”

properties.name: Kuching
properties.note: Tony returned to the small village of Kuching nearly a decade since his last visit. “When I first went up this river, I was sick with love—the bad kind, the fist around your heart kind. I ran far, but there was no escaping it. It followed me up river, all the way. That was 10 long years ago. … Yet here I am again, heading up to that same longhouse in the jungle.”

properties.name: Corazon de Tierra
properties.note: 100% Baja cuisine - local ingredients. Roasted tuna belly with daikon and kohlarbi, nasturtium; leg of lamb braised in black bean with swiss chard, carrot, radish, and turnip.

properties.name: Cantina El Danubio
properties.note: Tony had drinks with poet and activist Javier Sicilia.

properties.name: La Guerrerense
properties.note: World famous tostada food cart. Fresh scallop with purple onion; Salt cod with onion, olives, tomato, chiles, abuelita sauce; octopus with red snapper ceviche, and avocado

properties.name: TACOS LILY
properties.note: Tony had Baja fish tacos; there is a condiment bar with different kinds of salsas, crema, guacamole, and other toppings

properties.name: Calle Bajío & Chilpancingo
properties.note: Site of Dona Anastasia's blue corn quesadillas stuffed with various fillings. It's a food stand on the corner.

properties.name: Cantina La Mascota
properties.note: Tony met with Carlos, who took over his job as head chef at Les Halles. Breakfast with tequila. Food is free with drinks.

properties.name: Escuela de Box Rudy Perez
properties.note: Tony visited this boxing gym to speak with boxer Jorge Lacierva

properties.name: Fonda Margarita
properties.note: Family style breakfast. Huevos rancheros, beans, tortitas, stews, chicharron

properties.name: Máximo Bistrot
properties.note: Tony ate in Chef Eduardo Garcia's kitchen at this restaurant, one of the hottest places in Mexico. He ate abalone with chiles, lemon, and brown butter; confit of suckling pig, "topped with grandma’s salsa and served on a tortilla."

properties.name: MIGAS La Guera
properties.note: Located in a large public market in the neighborhood of Tepito, which is known to be a rough area to live in. Tony and hosts had migas: Cracked ham bones stewed with garlic, onion, cascabel chiles, and epazote; thickened with stale bread and served with tortillas.

properties.name: Pulquería Las Duelistas
properties.note: Not sure if this is the exact pulqueria is (where they serve pulque, an alcoholic cactus sap drink) and some sort of Mexican moonshine

properties.name: Tacos El Huequito
properties.note: Arguably the best al pastor tacos in the city

properties.name: Monterrey
properties.note: Tony dropped off a package for the sushi chef named Alfredo at Laredo Sushi Roll. Alfredo's mother cooked them Mexican food

properties.name: Taqueria El Compadre
properties.note: Best pork tacos ever

properties.name: Central de Abastos Oaxaca
properties.note: Chef Alejandro Ruiz Olmedo and Tony visited this Central Market where they ate barbacoa tacos, consommé, and tlayudas (perhaps best described as a Oaxacan pizza).

properties.name: Espacio Zapata
properties.note: Tony spoke with street artist Yescka about revolutionary art.

properties.name: El Mexico de Ayer
properties.note: Tony had a family style meal with Presidio Mayor John Ferguson, his daughter Molly, her boyfriend, and the former mayor of Ojinaga

properties.name: Piedras Negras
properties.note: Nachos were invented here

properties.name: Hotel San Francisco
properties.note: Tony ate worms and ant eggs. Would not do it again.

visits.title: Puebla, Where the Good Cooks Are From

properties.name: Monte Albán
properties.note: The Zapotec ruins are here. Tony and his guide drank mescal here.

properties.name: Restaurante Tlamanalli
properties.note: Tony visited a small town called Teotitlán del Valle and met Abigail Mendoza, a descendant from pre-Spanish Mexicans. She cooked a Chicken mole for him, using 35 kinds of chiles. The dish takes 2 weeks to prepare. She also made cow and pork brains cooked with chiles, tomatoes, and yerba santa.

properties.name: Dandy del Sur Cantina
properties.note: Tony had a drink with a guy named Tony who is a nightlife promoter.

properties.name: La Mezcalera
properties.note: Tony said he felt old here but liked tasting the mezcal

properties.name: Las Ahumaderas
properties.note: Tony had tacos here

properties.name: Mision 19
properties.note: High end Mexican regional cuisine. Very old recipes, reinvented.

properties.name: Terraza Vallarta
properties.note: They had micheladas and seafood. "Pescado empapelado (grilled and seasoned red snapper heaped with vegetables). Brochettes of bacon wrapped shrimp stuffed with crabmeat."

properties.name: Central Marketplace
properties.note: Bought beef for kefta and tagine to make

properties.name: Dar Diafa دار الضيافة‎
properties.note: Traditional Moroccan feast

properties.name: Fes
properties.note: Center of Moroccan cuisine

properties.name: Sahara Desert Morocco
properties.note: They trekked out to the desert and roasted a whole lamb in the sand.

properties.name: Bocadillo Abdelmalik
properties.note: Tony had a tuna bocadillo here: Spanish style sandwiches with tuna, veggies, and hard-cooked eggs, a healthy wad of mayo, and a crispy layer of french fries in the sandwich.

properties.name: Boutique Majid
properties.note: Antique shop Tony visited and bought a mortar & pestle style tool from the Gond tribe in Mali for $300.

properties.name: Café Baba
properties.note: A cafe serving hashish. "There is one particular cafe in the heart of the kasbah that's drawn in foreign dignitaries, rock stars, aristocrats, and artists since it opened its doors in 1943," Tony explained. Afterward they went directly across the street to a vendor selling spanish omelets filled with potatoes, eggs, ketchup, and mayonnaise.

properties.name: Café Tinjis
properties.note: Tony had espresso with Jonathan Dawson, British journalist

properties.name: Grand Socco
properties.note: Tony met with photographer Cherie Nutting and went to this outdoor food market. Tony bought fresh goat cheese wrapped in palm leaves and some flatbread for breakfast.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony visited this small village town at the base of the Rif mountains and had dinner family style with Bachir Attar, leader of The Master Musicians of Jajouka and his family. Braywine (handformed dough filled with seasoned beef, baked until golden, then crisped in oil); chicken tagine; a wild mountain spinach dish with garlic, cilantro, hot and black peppers, lemon, and olive oil; dessert of fruit and mint tea.

properties.name: Restaurant Andalus
properties.note: Tony ate lunch at this locals only place, with Majid, the owner of Boutique Majid. Tomatoes brushed with local olive oil, garlic, and coriander; beef liver kebabs grilled over charcoal; swordfish; orange roughy.

properties.name: Saveur de Poisson
properties.note: Restaurant serving tagine. Tony had fresh olives; roasted walnuts; warm bread; tagine with baby shark, calamari, and monkfish with fresh mountain spinach; shark kebabs; strawberries, pine nuts, and honey

properties.name: Tangier
properties.note: Tony visited Christopher Gibbs' house (antiques dealer and expat) for a garden dinner party with other expats.

properties.name: Mercado de Peixe
properties.note: They bought seafood here, then brought it to Esplanada Belinha to have it cooked. Clams steamed with garlic; prawns grilled and basted with butter with nshima (thick porridge made of ground maize).

properties.name: Copacabana
properties.note: Tony had piri piri chicken here. It is a spicy grilled chicken basted with piri piri chili sauce (piri piri peppers, lime, peanut oil, minced garlic, tomato).

properties.name: Bagan Palace Ruins
properties.note: Tony and Philippe visited the palace ruins and admired the structures

properties.name: Old Bagan
properties.note: Tony and Philippe traveled on a train through the Burmese country to Bagan. They bought snacks from locals at the train station. The train ride was very bumpy.

properties.name: Sarabha Restaurant
properties.note: Tony ate at a small food stand near the Bagan Palace ruins and called it the best restaurant in the country so far. He had chicken curry and raved about all the small accompaniments served.

properties.name: 19th Street Night Market
properties.note: Tony met with a local punk band at this night market.

properties.name: Feel Myanmar
properties.note: Tony met with culinary writer Ma Thanegi. They had pig’s-head salad with Kaffir lime leaf, long-bean salad with sesame and fish sauce, pennywort-leaf salad, a salad of Indian-style samosa.

properties.name: Kaung Myat Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and the punk band ate barbecue at this stand in the night market.

properties.name: Min Lan Seafood (Parami Branch)
properties.note: Tony had prawn curry here and cringed at the local cultural norm of making kissing noises to call a waiter over.

properties.name: Morning Star Tea House
properties.note: Not sure if this location still exists, the CNN site says it is on Saya San Road, but not showing up on the map. Tony met with San Zarni Bo and had fermented tea leaf salad, which is served with peanuts, lime, and chilies.

properties.name: Seit Tine Kya
properties.note: Tea shop where Tony had morning tea with U Thiha Saw, a journalist. They had the hot black tea with a dollop of sweetened condensed milk and a fish soup.

properties.name: Sofaer & Co
properties.note: "Once one of the swankest department stores in Rangoon," says Tony. "Now people live here."

properties.name: Taung Htate Pan Street
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Philippe Lajaunie, restaurateur, former owner of Brasserie Les Halles. They had chicken necks and cold beer.

properties.name: Yangon
properties.note: Tony visited this town during Thadingyut, the Full Moon festival celebrated by Buddhists at the end of October. He ate street food and watched the human-powered ferris wheels

properties.name: Oshetu Community Market
properties.note: Street food market

properties.name: 't Aepjen
properties.note: Beer

properties.name: Albert Cuyp Market
properties.note: STROOPWAFEL!

properties.name: Beer Tasting Room In The Wildeman
properties.note: Sausage, cheese, beer

properties.name: Café Soundgarden
properties.note: Beer

properties.name: De Pilsener Club (Engelse Reet)
properties.note: Beer

properties.name: De Twee Zwaantjes
properties.note: Bar

properties.name: Electric Ladyland - Museum of Fluorescent Art
properties.note: Trippy art

properties.name: Frens Haringhandel
properties.note: Salted Pickled Herring

properties.name: Gebr. Hartering
properties.note: Simple seasonal food

properties.name: Maloe Melo
properties.note: Dive bar

properties.name: Original Dampkring
properties.note: Coffee and marijuana cafe

properties.name: Pancakehouse Upstairs
properties.note: Pancakes that inspired a Beastie Boys song

properties.name: Paradox
properties.note: Marijuana cafe

properties.name: Restaurant Blauw aan de Wal
properties.note: Mediterranean dishes using fresh, local ingredients

properties.name: Tempo Doeloe
properties.note: Indonesian food

properties.name: Three Little Bottles
properties.note: Kinda like gin, an alcohol made with juniper

properties.name: Vondelpark
properties.note: Public park

properties.name: Warung Spang Makandra
properties.note: Roti, kebabs, sandwiches

properties.name: Flor De Pino
properties.note: Run by a woman who has cooked for nearly every important political figure. No longer open to the public. Some dishes include: Carne de Monte (iguana) and bull nuts.

properties.name: Leche agria El Ganadero
properties.note: Tony called this place El Vaquero. A soured milk yogurt drink is supposed to be a hangover cure. Nacatamales is corn masa inside plantain leaves filled with pork, tomato, and herbs. Tony said it was the best meal he ate so far in the trip.

properties.name: Managua
properties.note: After their shoot was canceled when authorities did not want them filming street racing, Tony and his driver and friends went to a hamburger stand on the street for burgers and hot dogs.

properties.name: Terminal de Autobuses Mercado Roberto Huembes
properties.note: Market, depot, and food court. Tony and his friend Hector ate a platter of beef & onions with rice

properties.name: Vaho tradicional
properties.note: Cassava and plantain cooked slowly in banana leaves with marinated beef with garlic, onions, tomato, and bitter orange, layered with green onions and red peppers.

properties.name: Andela Nigeria
properties.note: Tony visited this training program where people are taught coding and software engineering.

properties.name: Central Mosque - Hausa Community Agege
properties.note: Tony met with His Royal Highness, Alhaji Musa Muhammadu Dogonkadai, Sarkin Hausawan Agege. They had masa (griddle cakes made from sticky rice, sugar, potash—a mineral-rich salt), fura (millet and milk compote), danwake (bean-flour dumplings served with tomatoes), moringa salad, kuli-kuli powder (ground peanut).

properties.name: Computer Village
properties.note: Tony met with an economics expert to talk about the financial ecosystem of this marketplace. Young people learn to take apart and fix phones for a living.

properties.name: Iya-Eba Restaurant & Bar
properties.note: Tony had spicy pepper soup with journalist Kadaria Ahmed

properties.name: New Afrika Shrine
properties.note: Tony met the owner of this musical venue

properties.name: Rue80
properties.note: Tony had drinks with Shina Peller, owner of Club Quilox, (a music venue) and Banky Wellington, record label owner

properties.name: Stellas Kitchen
properties.note: Tony visited this restaurant to have pounded yams, egusi soup, "a stew of goat meat, melon seeds, fish stock, and chilies."

properties.name: Yakoyo Food Restaurant
properties.note: Traditional Nigerian foods such as ewedu (jute leaf) soup with crayfish, chilies, and locust beans; jollof rice (cooked in tomato sauce); goat; pounded yam.

properties.name: Al Hamra
properties.note: A catering business run by women whose profits go to funding children's education in rural areas. They cooked a meal of "kabuli laham, slow cooked goat in a rice pilaf scented with star anise. Musanif djaj, a local specialty, are pan seared dumplings stuffed with chicken, pepper, ginger, turmeric, and onions. And of course there's Omani bread with honey."

properties.name: Sharqiya Sands
properties.note: Tony camped out here in the desert and ate a meal with locals. They cooked the meal on the fire.

properties.name: Bait Al Luban Restaurant
properties.note: "Dishes like pakora and kachori which both came back from India, chapati from East Africa, and shuwa, Oman's classic special event dish.... they slather a goat with a spicy paste consisting of cumin, coriander, red pepper, cinnamon, cardamom, and nutmeg. Then wrap the meat in palm or banana leaves, dig a hole, throw in some meat, and cover it up and leave underground for a day or two over hot coals."

properties.name: Mutrah Corniche
properties.note: "I love that word, corniche. I love any city with a corniche," said Tony.

properties.name: Mutrah Souq
properties.note: Tony and Aisha Stoby, PhD candidate of Middle Eastern art, strolled through this market and talked about the culture of Oman.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had dinner of salmon marinated in pomegranate juice at a home in this town with Amichai Luria and Amiad Cohen

properties.name: Alrowwad Cultural and Arts Societyجمعية الرواد للثقافة والفنون‎
properties.note: Palestinian children from the Aida Refugee Camp participate in a theater production here

properties.name: West Bank
properties.note: Tony visited the Palestinian side, referred to as the West Bank or the Samaria, Palestine, or Judea.

properties.name: Bethlehem
properties.note: Tony visited Aida Refugee camp and had a meal at the martyr center.

properties.name: Gaza
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Laila El-Haddad and her family. They ate maqluba (a traditional Palestinian dish made of layers of fried eggplant, tomato, potatoes, caramelized onions, and chicken, sautéed then simmered in a broth with nutmeg, cinnamon, cardamom, and rice). They traveled just outside Gaza City to a gathering place for elders where they had fatit ’ajir (roasted baby watermelon, eggplant, tomatoes, squash, chiles, and olive oil) served with unleavened bread.

properties.name: Ramallah
properties.note: On the edge of this town, Tony visited an all-women racing team

properties.name: Eli
properties.note: Tony and guide stopped to view this settlement and talked about the conflicts between Palestinians and Jews

properties.name: Kwang Chow Chinese Dive
properties.note: Old school Cantonese Panamanian food. Roast duck, wontons.Westernized Chinese food.

properties.name: Mercado de Mariscos
properties.note: Tony had extremely fresh ceviche at this fish market

properties.name: Sorolo
properties.note: Local seafood known for their fresh marinated fish, fried. And soup.

properties.name: Iglesia Divino Niño Jesus en Asuncion Paraguay
properties.note: Tony visited this old cemetery to see if he could find his great great great great grandfather's grave. He did not find it but historians said it might have been overbuilt. While in Paraguay he learned that his ancestor came into the country as a hat maker but also sold fireworks, which could have been made into weapons.

properties.name: Lido Bar
properties.note: "Ladies in orange vests cook and serve old school Paraguayan working class food to people from every walk of life," Tony explains. They ate empanadas de carna (beef, onion, and hard-cooked egg).

properties.name: Mercado Cuatro
properties.note: A food market with food stands, known as the largest and most popular food market. Tony ate a catfish soup, gnocchi and stew, sopa de Paraguayan.

properties.name: Marañón Province
properties.note: Tony and Eric stopped at a roadside stand in the Marañón canyon to have sopa de gallina, a Peruvian hen soup. After they went to the cacao plantation, they had dinner at Don Fortunato's home: juane (rice dumplings with boiled egg, chicken, and achiote) and roasted cuy (guinea pig) in a cacao sauce

properties.name: Mercado Municipal Huacrachuco
properties.note: Unsure if this is the correct location. There was a scene on the show where Tony and Eric visited Mercado Central on Calle San Martin in the Marañón Canyon where they bought food to cook to feed their host, Don Elioberto, and his family. They made chicken and shrimp with peppers and onions, along with mashed potatoes and traditional Aztec hot chocolate. I could not find a Mercado Central on a street with this name on the map.

properties.name: Chiclayo
properties.note: The staging area for Tony and Eric's visit to the mountains to find cacao for their company making chocolate bars.

properties.name: Mercado Modelo De Chiclayo
properties.note: Eric took Tony shopping in this market to find supplies for a shaman they will visit later in the episode.

properties.name: ámaZ Restaurante
properties.note: Tony and Eric visited this restaurant run by Chef Pedro Escofino, highlighting Peruvian ingredients. They started with a lime cocktail with culantro; breadfruit with tuna and cashew sauce; scallops with wild almond and freshwater shrimp dashi; Inchcape soup made of ham, peanuts, and corn; patarashca (catfish wrapped in leaves and cooked on hot coals); paiche (a type of fish from the Amazon River) atop a palm fruit purée with a fermented yucca sauce and a chili pepper sauce made with nuts and ants. Eric did not like the ants.

properties.name: Anticuchos Doña Pochita
properties.note: Tony and Eric stopped at this food stand specializing in beef heart, marinated with garlic, cumin, onion, and vinegar and grilled.

properties.name: Chez Wong
properties.note: Tony and Eric visited this restaurant where the chef uses a wok to cook. They had octopus and flounder ceviche; tiradito of flounder with pecan, lime, ají limo chili peppers, and sesame oil.

properties.name: Larco Museum
properties.note: Tony and Eric visited this museum of pre-Columbian Peruvian artifacts. While there, they went to the erotic gallery and looked at erotic pottery.

properties.name: Peña Don Porfirio, Barranco
properties.note: Criollo restaurant

properties.name: Pucusana
properties.note: Coque Ossio, one of the most successful chefs in Peru, spends weekends in Pucusana, a small fishing village. His mom, Marisa Guiulfo, is like Peru's Julia Child. They made food and pisco sours for Tony and Eric Ripert. Marisa opens the house every weekend for a large group to visit and eat.

properties.name: Restaurant SONIA
properties.note: Ceviche restaurant

properties.name: Pochero Kinaraan
properties.note: Stuffed Crab and pork shank soup (called bulalo)

properties.name: Bale Dutung
properties.note: Chef's Claude Tayag home which is also a gallery, restaurant, and school. There are 4 kitchens. The chef prepared food in the outdoor kitchen.

properties.name: Mila's Kambingan
properties.note: Goat offal prepared many ways. Sinigang was served

properties.name: Mad Dog Motorcycle Club Philippines
properties.note: Tony watched cover bands at this biker bar

properties.name: Alvie
properties.note: Tony had halo halo, which translates to "mix mix." It is an "icy, milky, technicolor concoction of sundry sweet mung beans, candied fruits, and gelatins." Also has banana, condensed milk, and sweet young coconut.

properties.name: Chinatown
properties.note: They went to food street in Chinatown. They had chicken balls and pancit palabok

properties.name: Oarhouse Pub of Manila
properties.note: Tony met with journalist Mark Navales to have drinks at this bar.

properties.name: Pearl Garden Hotel
properties.note: Tony crashed a holiday party here, the Seven Continents Travel & Tours corporate Christmas party. They had lechon, a whole pig roast pork that is traditional during the holidays.

properties.name: Super Six
properties.note: Tony came here to eat his favorite Filipino street food, which he describes as "possibly the best thing you can ever eat with a cold beer." The dish he is talking about is sisig, a sizzling dish of pig face with a runny egg on top.

properties.name: Dampa Sa Farmers Market
properties.note: A seafood market where you can buy ingredients and someone will cook it for you. They had pinakbet, shrimp adobo, and coconut chili crab

properties.name: Meal with a local
properties.note: Tony had another meal with a local, the nanny of one of the show's directors, Erik Osterholm. She made kare kare, or oxtail soup. After living abroad for over 30 years, Aurora is finally back home living with her kids and grandchildren, although many still live abroad to send money home to support the family. They talked about the sacrifices Aurora and many other Filipinos make to send money for better lives for these poor families.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony met with the local band Keystone. They made him the national dish, adobo. They had adobo with pork and chicken.

properties.name: Jollibee Bluebay Walk
properties.note: Tony loves this local fast food joint, even though he said "I hate myself" as he was finishing his meal. He had Jolly Spaghetti (with hot dogs in it) and Chicken Joy with a brown sauce.

properties.name: Peter Café Sport
properties.note: Gin & tonics and snacks

properties.name: A Ginjinha
properties.note: A liqueur made from morello cherries, ginjinha has been sold from this spot for 70 years

properties.name: Alma
properties.note: After shopping at the fish market, Chef Henrique Sa Pessoa makes Tony a meal. Mackerel, pork with sweet potato purée served over bok choy, a semi-salted cod called Bacalhau with chickpea purée.

properties.name: Bistro 100 Maneiras
properties.note: Modern take on traditional Portuguese dishes. They tried deep-fried codfish tripe; game pie with truffles, rabbit, and São Jorge cheese; horse heart and other offal; and cabrito (milk-fed young goat) served on rice mixed with the goat liver and tongue, mushroom, onion, and chicken.

properties.name: Cantinho do Avillez
properties.note: Tony had lunch here with a famous Portuguese musician. They sampled peixihos da horta (tempura green beans), morcela (blood sausage), pig trotters with cilantro, a golden egg dish with cepes (mushroom) and cheese, and a hazelnut dessert.

properties.name: Market Alvalade Norte
properties.note: Large fish market where Tony and Chef Henrique Sa Pessoa visited. They also have produce, cheese, and meat.

properties.name: O Trevo
properties.note: Tony had beer and bifana, a sandwich with thin slices of marinated pork, with mustard and chili oil.

properties.name: Ramiro
properties.note: A local favorite. Modernizing traditional Portuguese food. They started with seafood: Barnacles, shrimp, crab, clams, they ended with the steak sandwich.

properties.name: Sol e Pesca
properties.note: Tony met with a local band to try food from this fishing store turned restaurant that serves canned seafood from the area.

properties.name: Tasca do Chico
properties.note: Tony met with a writer and a Fado singer in this bar to discuss the revolutionary period of the country

properties.name: Marisqueira Antiga
properties.note: Tony had an enormous platter of seafood consisting of "coastal shrimp, local oysters and crab, sea urchin, langoustine, gooseneck barnacles, whelks, and clams, and a salt-baked sea bass."

properties.name: Real Companhia Velha
properties.note: Tony tried port and assorted cheeses, bread, pumpkin jam.

properties.name: A Cozinha Do Martinho
properties.note: Tony had fried sardines, Tripe Porto-style, and wine.

properties.name: Bakery and Pastry Shop "Bolhão"
properties.note: Salt cod, Portuguese national dish

properties.name: Café Restaurante O Afonso
properties.note: Tony had a francesinha (a sandwich made of sliced bread, steak, sausage, linguiça, cooked in a sauce of beer, port wine, spice, tomato, and a lot of cheese melted on top).

properties.name: Cervejaria Gazela - O Original Cachorrinho
properties.note: Tony had cachorros here, made of toasted bread, fresh pork sausage, cheese, and a spicy sauce. And fries

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony witnessed the town get together to slaughter a pig and cook it for a feast.

properties.name: Porto
properties.note: Tony came here to have some roast pig

properties.name: Restaurante Casa Aleixo
properties.note: Specializes in octopus

properties.name: Restaurante Rogério do Redondo
properties.note: Seafood restaurant

properties.name: Casa de Pasto Flor
properties.note: Local wines and petiscos (Portuguese tapas)

properties.name: Lechonera Los Pinos
properties.note: Roasted pork

properties.name: Casa Vieja
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Liza Fournier Cordova, a teacher. They had corn fritters with ham; plantain soup; pastel al caldero, or "pork marinated in bitter orange, taro root, green plantains, squash, and garbanzo beans, slow cooked"; morcilla (blood sausage).

properties.name: El Rancho de Apa
properties.note: Tony had slow roasted suckling pig with ranch owner Apa Ramos. They also had gandinga, a stew made from pig heart, liver, and kidney.

properties.name: Jobos Beach
properties.note: Sonia Rican restaurant was here before; Closed now, Tony had mofongo there.

properties.name: Salvemos a Playuela
properties.note: Tony met with activist "Pocho" to have a feast of Sancocho, a beef stew with yuca, plantains; fresh-caught grouper and red snapper.

properties.name: Barrachina
properties.note: Where the pina colada was invented. (Tony talked crap about it)

properties.name: Esquina El Watusi
properties.note: Tony had drinks with Hermes Ayala, freelance journalist for outlets including Vice and HuffPost; Laura Moscoso, journalist specializing in data visualization and multimedia.

properties.name: Raíces
properties.note: Traditional Puerto Rican food

properties.name: El Trasmayo
properties.note: Tony met with chef and entrepreneur Pedro Álvarez Cortez at this crab shack. They had crab, coconut arepas, and beer.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had sauteed chicken gizzards with green bananas, codfish fritters, carne guisada, and mofongo with the band Fiel a la Vega.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate at Chef Erica Connelly's home with her family. She made lobster, coconut arepas, and conch salad.

properties.name: Puerto de Vieques
properties.note: Tony had a meal on a boat to Vieques. They ate salted cod marinated in coconut milk and seared, root vegetables, escabeche, and avocado sala

properties.name: Jariștea
properties.note: Bucharest style meal. They had a whole suckling pig. Tony hated the themed Imperialist atmosphere but loved the food

properties.name: Sighetu Marmației
properties.note: Tony visited a local men's bar called Cafe Astoria in this town, but it doesn't seem to be open anymore

properties.name: Gorki
properties.note: Very high end restaurant

properties.name: 16 Tonn
properties.note: Tony had drinks with Travis Link, manager of punk rock band Louna, and Rouben Kazariyan

properties.name: Kazansky railway station
properties.note: Tony and Zamir took the luxury Grand Express train from Moscow to St. Petersburg. Aboard the train, they had blini with caviar and cold pickled herring, and vodka.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony met with Alexander Lebedev, businessman and owner of opposition newspaper Novaya Gazeta, at his home. They had fresh potato chips and Scottish salmon with cherry-tree sawdust.

properties.name: Metropol Hotel
properties.note: Tony met up with Zamir and ate Astrakhan caviar, salted cucumber with honey, Baltic sprat with beetroot, muksun (white fish with maldon salt), and vodka (of course)

properties.name: Russkiy, Restoran
properties.note: A fancy restaurant

properties.name: The State Tretyakov Gallery
properties.note: Very high end shopping

properties.name: Yornik
properties.note: Tony and Zamir had a meal with Boris Nemtsov, one of the main critics of Putin, who served as an advisor under Yeltsin (he has since been assassinated in 2015, and this restaurant is now closed).

properties.name: Вернисаж в Измайлово
properties.note: The Izmailovo Market - a huge flea market. Also has food stands with grilled meats on a stick

properties.name: Cococo
properties.note: Former site of restaurant called Cococo. Tony and Zamir had a meal with Sergey Shnurov and Matilda Shnurova, co-owners of Cococo.

properties.name: Petropavlovskaya Krepost'
properties.note: Former site of a fortress built to protect the city from marauding Swedes, it was taken over by Bolshevik rebels.

properties.name: The Loft Club
properties.note: Tony met with Xena Robrek, an openly gay artist and filmmaker, and Darya Tarasova, a local fixer and translator at this club to have drinks.

properties.name: Restoran "Balaklava"
properties.note: Tony called this restaurant Tatchaninmis. Tony and Zamir drank several bottles of vodka and ate seafood here.

properties.name: Dyagilevo
properties.note: Dance club

properties.name: Catherine Palace
properties.note: Seeing this building makes you understand why the revolution happened

properties.name: Church of the Resurrection of Christ Marker
properties.note: One of the last czars or Russia, Alexander II, was blown up at this very spot by a terrorist group

properties.name: Kuznechnyy Rynok
properties.note: Fresh produce, meats, pickled veggies and fruits

properties.name: Podvor'ye
properties.note: Traditional country style Russian food. Russian dumplings, potato pancakes, cabbage

visits.title: The Cook Who Came In From the Cold

properties.name: Souvenirs Shop
properties.note: Many stands selling wares

properties.name: Ресторан Идиотъ
properties.note: Blintzy - a thin pancake filled with savory or sweet fillings. Tony had one with yogurt and dried apricot.

properties.name: Sun Beach & Eat Seckie & Vanessa
properties.note: Ingredients grown in garden or locally, and cocktails

properties.name: Tante Pearls Resturant
properties.note: Tony was hungover from eating manicou stew (opossum stew) on the beach and drinking strong rum. He ate here the next morning: Caribbean Conch Curry, Souse soup

properties.name: Bully's Bar
properties.note: There is (was?) a bar in this area called Bully's Bar, can't find it on the map anymore. A local bar, people told Tony it was in a "bad part of town" but he went anyway and said everyone was lovely

properties.name: Captain Gourmet
properties.note: Tony picked up food supplies here for a boat trip

properties.name: Happy Island
properties.note: A man-made island made of conch shells with a bar and cocktails

properties.name: Freedom Fighter's Ital Shack
properties.note: Healthy fresh vegetarian food, fresh juices

visits.title: Food Tastes Better with Sand Between your Toes

properties.name: Gus's Beach Bar
properties.note: This was Tony's favorite restaurant on earth. It closed. He was very happy here.

visits.title: Food Tastes Better with Sand Between your Toes

properties.name: Johnny B's Under the Tree
properties.note: He moved since the episode aired. This is the new location. Serves ribs and other Caribbean BBQ

visits.title: Food Tastes Better with Sand Between your Toes

properties.name: Poulet D' Orleans
properties.note: Caribbean/Creole seafood

visits.title: Food Tastes Better with Sand Between your Toes

properties.name: Souq Minara - سوق المنارة‎
properties.note: Tony and Danya ate an offal breakfast at a place called Abu Abdullah's. Tony liked it more than she did.

properties.name: AlBaik
properties.note: Fast food - fried chicken with garlic sauce

properties.name: Dibiterie Le Mboté
properties.note: Tony ate with rapper Djily Bagdad. They ate dibi, roasted meat served on paper with grilled onions and mustard.

properties.name: Huitres De Sokone
properties.note: Tony met with a group of women (Fama Diof, photographer; Umi Nador, journalist and TV presenter; Minyel Tal, entrepreneur) to discuss gender roles in a mostly Muslim country. They ate clams, sea urchins, and thiof fish.

properties.name: Lac Rose
properties.note: The pink lake, as it is called, is a site for harvesting salt, but is also known as the finish line "for a legendary trans-African endurance race." Tony sat with a driver as they did some offroading then had a meal together.

properties.name: Le jardin de l'Amitié
properties.note: Tony met with a group of entrepreneurs to discuss the economy of Senegal to have drinks.

properties.name: Marche Kermel
properties.note: Tony met at this bustling food market with NPR West Africa correspondent Ofeibea Quist-Arcton.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Not sure if this is the correct location. Tony said the beach was called "Coeur. de Cassazione" but I could not find this on the map. He ate with Pierre Thiam, a renowned chef and cookbook author. They had yassa, made with a local fish called ciof, "cooked simply on the bone, wrapped in foil, and steamed over the fire, served with a mignonette style sauce with onions; grilled mussels, sea urchin."

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate the Senegal national dish with some locals. The national dish is called ceebu jen (meaning rice with fish). The fish they used was "Senegalese grouper, scored and stuffed with a mixture of garlic, parsley, and peppers, then slow simmered in a hearty tomato broth, infused by a funkatizing goodness brought by fermented conch and salt fish, served over rice and vegetables."

properties.name: Restaurant le BAZOFF
properties.note: Tony watched local musician Youssou N’Dour perform at this restaurant. They also ate beef mafe served with rice, a stew thickened with ground peanuts. Tony says, "there are similar preparations throughout West Africa, but the Senegalese version is particularly great."

properties.name: Boulangerie SMBIS
properties.note: Tony had a street food dish called ndambe, a spread of spicy lentils on a baguette. You buy the baguette from this bakery, then go to the woman on the street who has the lentils to spread on it.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had a meal with locals. They ate mulet farci, a stuffed baked local fish called mullet, stuffed with bread and vegetables and baked.

properties.name: 2 Dickson Rd
properties.note: Wanderlust Hotel. Affordable/hipster

properties.name: 545 Whampoa Prawn Noodle
properties.note: Tony met with KF Seetoh, his guide in Singapore, "the guru behind the Makansutra guides and an advocate for the kind of democratic, affordable, casual chaos of the hawker world." They had prawn mi from this stand.

properties.name: Adam Road Food Centre
properties.note: Hawker food center. Have at least 3 meals at any one of these food centers! Indian, Malay, Chinese food.

properties.name: Airport Boulevard
properties.note: Best airport in the world. Fern garden, shopping, great food, rooftop pool, bars

properties.name: Arab Street
properties.note: Tea, cafes, Malay food, nightlife.

properties.name: Aurum Land Private Limited
properties.note: Molecular gastronomy in a "hospital chic" themed restaurant. You are seated in a wheelchair. Weird.

properties.name: Bar Stories
properties.note: Fancy cocktails with fresh fruits

properties.name: Bismillah Biryani Restaurant One North
properties.note: Indian food. Spices are ground fresh here.

properties.name: Candlenut
properties.note: The world’s first Michelin-starred Peranakan restaurant, Candlenut takes a contemporary yet authentic approach to traditional Straits-Chinese cuisine

properties.name: Chin Chin Eating House
properties.note: Singaporean national dish: Chicken rice. Curry fish head stew, breaded pork cutlet

properties.name: Deen Tiga Rasa
properties.note: Mutton bones cooked in a sweet and spicy sauce. It's messy.

properties.name: Geylang Claypot Rice
properties.note: Crispy rice with chicken, mushroom, fish. String beans, lobster, crabs, clams.

properties.name: Geylang Serai
properties.note: Food market

properties.name: Goodwood Park Hotel
properties.note: Tea in the hotel restaurant

properties.name: Grand Hyatt Singapore
properties.note: Fancy hotel where Bourdain stayed. They have street food inside the hotel.

properties.name: Guan Hoe Soon Restaurant
properties.note: "The oldest Peranakan restaurant in Singapore, predating independence." They had a Laotian dish called ikam asam nana, a sour fish curry with pineapple; itek sio, a duck braised in tamarind and coriander; and okra sautéed with sambal.

properties.name: Hjh Maimunah Restaurant & Catering Pte Ltd
properties.note: Malaysian food: BBQ fish with onion/chili sauce, beef rendang, fried saltfish, beef liver, oxtail soup, stewed jackfruit

properties.name: Imperial Restaurant
properties.note: Herbal medicine + food

properties.name: International Muslim Food Stall Nasi Lemak
properties.note: Changi Village Hawker Centre, this food stall has egg, chicken or fish and rice, sambal, coconut rice.

properties.name: Keng Eng Kee Seafood
properties.note: Tony had a meal with the family who runs this restaurant. They had pig trotters with black vinegar (and ginger, fried chilies, and asam), Hainan mixed vegetables (cabbage and glass noodles stir fried with bean curd, black mushrooms, pig skin, and shrimp).

properties.name: Kilo Lounge
properties.note: Italian Japanese. Prawn & chili pizza, mango poke.

properties.name: Lorong 29 Geylang
properties.note: Tony had a meal with Sadir Varaket, Melanie Chan, and Tanya Andoor. They had fried oyster omelets and fried prawns with chili paste; bak chor mi, a fish ball noodle soup; and beef satay.

properties.name: Mustafa Shopping Centre Singapore
properties.note: HUGE gift store

properties.name: Outram Park Fried Kway Teow Mee
properties.note: One of Tony's favorite things to eat in Singapore is char kway teow. "Ordinarily this is a breakfast," Tony explains. "I eat it every time I'm in Singapore." It is made of crispy lard bits, cockles, shrimp paste, and noodles.

properties.name: Sabar Menanti II
properties.note: Tony met with Singaporean- Malay entertainer and producer Najip Ali for Lontong (rice cake, coconut gravy, vegetables and hardboiled egg) and Mee Siam (fried rice noodles with chili paste, prawns, bean sprouts, and a spicy sweet and sour gravy, fried bean curd, and egg).

properties.name: Samy's Curry Restaurant
properties.note: Southern Indian food

properties.name: Singapore Flyer
properties.note: $25 to ride the tallest ferris wheel in the world

properties.name: Singapore River Cruise
properties.note: Bumboat tour

properties.name: SkyPark
properties.note: Best view of the city

properties.name: Sungei Road Flea Market
properties.note: Oldest flea market in Singapore

properties.name: Tanglin Halt Food Centre
properties.note: Have a Peanut Pancake. They roast and grind their own peanuts.

properties.name: Tekka Centre
properties.note: Hawker center. Stretch tea. A pocket of dough filled with minced lamb, onion, egg.

properties.name: The Banana Leaf Apollo
properties.note: Indian food. Fish head curry

properties.name: The Fullerton Hotel Singapore
properties.note: Old world, British colonial style hotel

properties.name: Tian Jin Hai Seafood International Pte. Ltd.
properties.note: Tony ate steamed shark head and chili crab.

properties.name: Tian Tian Hainanese Chicken Rice
properties.note: Tony says the Maxwell Food Centre is the best hawker center, and this place has the best chicken rice in Singapore

properties.name: Tiong Bahru Food Centre
properties.note: Hawker food center. Have at least 3 meals at any one of these food centers! Indian, Malay, Chinese food.

properties.name: Hillbrow
properties.note: "A notoriously dangerous district" where Tony met with DJ Les.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate iftar (meal at sundown when fasting for Ramadan has broken) with the Rasdien family. Malaysian family, they prepare panang curry with beef, a chicken pie, traditional ramadan shake.

properties.name: Neighbourgoods Market
properties.note: Tony ate a Balkan burger here. It is a flattened ground beef patty with tons of cheese and your choice of toppings. There is a rooftop with a view where you can sit and eat.

properties.name: Sympathy's Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and DJ Les had fried chicken, greens, mieliepap, beets, and coleslaw.

properties.name: The Yeoville Dinner Club
properties.note: Chef Sanza cooked for Tony: A Nigerian dish of egusi and beef (cooked with melon and pumpkin seeds) futu (crumbly cornmeal porridge); basmati rice with rose water; achar (pickled eggplant and mango); Congolese cassava; and falafel. Menu changes daily.

properties.name: Soweto
properties.note: Tony watched a soccer game at Mawilies Inn (could not find on the map) in Soweto, a suburb of Johannesburg. The town name derives from an abbreviation of Southwestern Township. They ate lamb's head; mieliepap (sticky porridge made from maize); stewed beef, dumplings, stewed greens, carrots, beans, gravy.

properties.name: Maders Slaghuis
properties.note: A butcher that sells biltong, a salted dried meat. Afrikaner paraphernalia hangs in the shop, which bothered Tony. For restaurant, you can go to the meat counter and order cuts of meat with "monkey gland sauce" on the side (barbecue sauce).

properties.name: Pretoria
properties.note: Administrative center of South Africa and once "the heart of apartheid"

properties.name: Chef Choi Biryong’s U.S. Army barrack-converted studio
properties.note: Tony and Chef Biryong make budae jjigae on his TV show called Mukbang.

properties.name: Gwangjang Market
properties.note: Tony has ddukboki (spicy rice cakes), japchae (sweet-potato noodles), and soondae (blood sausage)

properties.name: Jeongdaepo
properties.note: Korean BBQ with a group of Korean businessmen

properties.name: Joseon Gimbap Restaurant
properties.note: Tony and Nari ate Korean BBQ

properties.name: Junco Music Town
properties.note: Much to Tony's horror, his business friends decided to go to this karaoke bar

properties.name: Noryangjin Fisheries Wholesale Market
properties.note: Huge fish market

properties.name: PeaK PC Bang Yeoksam
properties.note: Tony played a video game in this PC video game cafe

properties.name: Beach barbecue
properties.note: Tony went to a moraga (a beach barbecue) with chefs Juan Andres Moria and Rafael Luca, and friends. They had dried tuna on toast with spicy olive oil, fish, cheese, pate, grilled octopus, sea bream, pork tenderloin, and morcilla (blood sausage).

properties.name: Asador Etxebarri
properties.note: "In every way, extraordinary" says Tony. He had lunch with his old friend Virginia Irizar, who runs a restaurant, and whose dad showed him San Sebastian for the first time. The chef of Etxebarri, Victor Arguinzoniz, makes everything himself from local ingredients.
