Tony had chorizo "served over a tiny little grill still smoking and infusing it with scent and flavor, grilled razor clams, beluga caviar... grilled and heaped over almond paste... perfectly cooked prawns. Line caught squid, grilled and served in its own ink. And king hell Galician beef chop, grilled."

properties.name: Tickets
properties.note: La vida tapa. Albert Adria is the chef. "Whimsical, theatrical; a carnival fairway-like feeling to its dining room," says Tony

properties.name: Bar Guillermina
properties.note: Tony and José Andrés had a bowl of beans with sausage and some cider. After they ate, they played a game similar to bowling.

properties.name: Sobrecueva
properties.note: Tony and José Andrés had Cabrales (a type of blue cheese) at this cheese farm. José commented that the cheese had penicillin in it and Tony's retort was, "I can feel my syphilis clearing already!"

properties.name: Casa Lin
properties.note: Tony and José Andrés had cider, sea urchin, spider crab, percebes (gooseneck barnacles)

properties.name: Mugaritz
properties.note: Renovated farmhouse turned restaurant of famous Spanish restaurant who trained at El Bulli, Andoni Aduriz

properties.name: El Bulli
properties.note: El Bulli was once located here, closed in 2011. The famous restaurant of the Adria brothers. Jose Andres (who trained at El Bulli) met up with Tony to eat there.

properties.name: Sidrería Asturias
properties.note: Tony and José had lunch with the man who caught el campanu (the season’s first caught salmon) in 2014. They had bread, salami, cheese, olives, beer.

properties.name: Bar Aliatar Los Caracoles
properties.note: Tony and his Director of Photography Zach Zamboni (who lives part time in Andalucia with his wife), ate a lunch of snails in almond sauce at this eatery with seating outdoors.

properties.name: Bar Gallardo
properties.note: Bar #3 for the group. They ate cheese, fried smelts, picarones (fried pastry), chulatitas (baby lamb chops).

properties.name: Casa Pasteles Albayzin
properties.note: Cafe con leche and a small breakfast like toast with tomato.

properties.name: El Tabernaculo
properties.note: Tony visited during the Holy Week and stopped here to have some sausage and drinks

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Zach's wife's family invited Tony for dinner. Iberico ham, remojon granadino (eaten during holy week, it has salt cod, egg, black olives, oranges, and tomatoes dressed in olive oil), migas (sardines, chorizo, melons, and peppers)

properties.name: Museo de La Zambra - Cueva de Curro
properties.note: In Sacromonte, a neighborhood made of cave structures. Tony watched a an jeurga "informal, intimate, and spontaneous performance"

properties.name: Restaurante Oliver
properties.note: Bar #2 for Tony, Zach, and Fuen. They had mussels steamed in butter and olive oil.

properties.name: Taberna La Tana
properties.note: A little place for tapas. Tony went with Zach and Fuen. They had salmon with tomato, caviar

properties.name: El Bohío
properties.note: Castillian food. Diego and Pepe (brothers) run this restaurant. modern twists on traditional dishes using hyper-local ingredients

properties.name: DiverXO
properties.note: Spanish seafood with Chinese influence

properties.name: La Gabinoteca
properties.note: Modern tapas. Foie gras with a sweet crispy pineapple topping; homemade veal sausage topped with housemade ketchup, cheese sauce, and crispy fried onions; gabas al ajillo (shrimp pounded thin and served with roe); chicken wings marinated in lime, honey, soy

properties.name: Mercado de San Miguel
properties.note: A century old market with fresh produce, seafood, locally made goods

properties.name: Restaurante Casa Salvador
properties.note: Spanish style restaurant, food like what Spaniards eat at home. Braised oxtail is the specialty

properties.name: San Mamés
properties.note: Famous for the Madrid Callos (tripe)

properties.name: Albaola Itsas Kultur Faktoria
properties.note: Xabier Agote, Shipwright and founder of the Basque Maritime Museum, is working here to built a 16th century style Basque galleon.

properties.name: Restaurante Güeyu Mar
properties.note: Tony and José had fresh caught grilled salmon with local moonshine. They laughed as they ate and drank outside at this place.

properties.name: Arzak
properties.note: Tony sat with the Arzaks, who, he considers family. "The food is innovative, wildly creative, forward thinking, but always, ALWAYS Basque. Marinated prawns on lemongrass and mint with beetroot and crunchy krill, roast pigeon with mastic and potato... grilled hake jowls with teff seeds and almonds served in a bamboo leaf... grilled monkfish with a pecan paste and hieroglyphics of pumpkin and sweet pea... white tuna with green melon and a jackfruit sauce... squid with walnut-ginger paste, psyllium sauce with black tomato."

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Bar restaurante Astelena
properties.note: Pastele de Pescado

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Bar Zeruko
properties.note: One of the many tapas places Tony ate at

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Casa Urola
properties.note: Tony ate here with Olatz González Abrisketa, a documentarian and professor of social anthropology at the University of the Basque Country. "Seared mushrooms with egg yolk and pine nuts... grilled tuna with marmitako sauce, a reference to a classic fishermen's stew; peas in a consommé of Ibérico ham," and squid with a white bean cream.

properties.name: Elkano Kalea
properties.note: Tony and Xabier had Rock prawn (head and body cooked separately—head grilled and body served semi-ceviche), grilled squid with onion-green pepper sauce, cocochas (hake fish jowls or chins), and grilled turbot (this is the dish people travel to this restaurant to try)

properties.name: Ganbara
properties.note: Tony went on a poteo (bar crawl) with the famous chef Juan Mari Arzak and his daughter Elena. They ate a lot of pintxos and drank beer. They did not specify the places they visited except for this one, because this was Tony's favorite place to get pintxos, and the first place someone brought him to when he first visited the Basque area. Iberico ham with mushrooms on toast, crab tartlets, seared wild mushrooms and foie gras with egg yolk (house specialty, and Tony's favorite)

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Haizea
properties.note: Tony had pinchos and Chacoli (a local sparkling wine that is very light and low in alcohol)

properties.name: Luis Irízar
properties.note: Culinary school

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Santa Lucía
properties.note: Churros and drinking chocolate

visits.title: San Sebastian: A Food Lover's Town

properties.name: Sociedad Aizepe
properties.note: Tony met with a group at this place. They discussed "txokos" or "private and historically all-male gastronomical societies, though that gender rule has changed with the times." The menu for the evening: "diced raw tuna, grilled prawns with green sauce" to start, then "for the main, fried hake cooked with txakoli and steamed clams."

properties.name: Ca'Laura
properties.note: Tony and Chef José Andrés at this plaza café. They had arvejas (green peas) with ham, croquette, mussels, steak topped with blue cheese

properties.name: Espinaler
properties.note: Clams, cockles, and other fresh seafood that the owner cans and sells. Expensive!

properties.name: Hacienda La Marquesa
properties.note: Tony visited La Marquesas Ranch, the private bullring of star matador David Fandila. Tony tried it himself with a calf. They then ate bull stew with local herbs, onions, and potatoes; ham; homemade chorizo; Spanish cheese; bread with olive oil

properties.name: Casa Eladia, Piñera, Rozaes
properties.note: Tony had a free-range chicken stew with rice called Pitu de Caleya.

properties.name: Galle Face Green
properties.note: "This is our Central Park," Dharshan Munidasa says (a chef and TV personality). Tony and Dharshan had street food here.

properties.name: Hotel De Pilawoos
properties.note: Late night dish called kottu roti. It is made of diced veggies, eggs, curry sauce, and chopped roti chopped together.

properties.name: Lakmali Hotel
properties.note: Tony had a heavy breakfast here although he was feeling very ill. Sambal and chili, rice.

properties.name: Nelum Kole Restaurant
properties.note: Tony had lunch at this local rice and curry joint with Kumar Lopez, CEO of Sri Lanka Press Institute, a news trade organization. They had chicken curry, spicy potatoes, beets, and rice.

properties.name: The Dutch Burgher Union of Ceylon
properties.note: Postcolonial elite. Tony describes it as "the last remnants of long-gone empires." They drank coconut liquor

properties.name: Wellawatte Railway Station
properties.note: Tony took a 10-hour train ride to Jaffna.

properties.name: Jaffna Beach
properties.note: Tony’s last meal in Sri Lanka is with local fishermen. They had grouper and squid with beer.

properties.name: Jaffna Railway Station
properties.note: Tony got off the train here in Jaffna, a coastal city "as far North as you can go before you hit India."

properties.name: Kali Kovil
properties.note: Tony visited during the Madai Festival for the Tamil people who are praying to Kali, a Hindu goddess.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate with Dr. Ravi Perumalpillai, his sister Jeeva Perumalpillai, and Punitham “aunty” Selvadura. The aunty made crab curry.

properties.name: Östermalms saluhall
properties.note: Traditional Swedish food; game birds - pheasant, goose, duck; seafood

properties.name: Lux Stockholm
properties.note: Fancy restaurant with a Michelin star

properties.name: Maxi Grillen
properties.note: Hot dogs

properties.name: Restaurant Pelikan
properties.note: Swedish traditional foods

properties.name: Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
properties.note: Honors a general that is an important figure in Taiwanese history

properties.name: Dihua Street
properties.note: One of the last parts of Taipei with remnants of colonial Taiwan. Shopping.

properties.name: Din Tai Fung Xinyi Restaurant
properties.note: Masters of the soup dumpling. This is a must try.

properties.name: Fengcheng Cantonese Restaurant
properties.note: Hakka cuisine from Southern China. The specialty is the "stinky tofu" (which Tony did not like)

properties.name: Fuzhou Pepper Buns (pork pepper buns)
properties.note: Hu Jiao Bing. Pork buns with peppers in a baked flour dough baked in a clay oven.

properties.name: Jin Chun Fa Beef Noodle
properties.note: National dish of Taiwan: Beef Noodle soup

properties.name: Keelung Night Market
properties.note: Crowded but one of the best markets in the city with tons of food stands. Try the rice pot sticker soup and oyster omelet. Also the Ba Wan (pork, shiitake, garlic, soy.

properties.name: Liu Mama Noodle
properties.note: Pork vermicelli soup

properties.name: Lungshan Temple
properties.note: One of the most famous temples in Taiwan, celebrates Buddhist, Taoist, and folk deities.

properties.name: MaoKong
properties.note: Mountain experience. Suburb south of the city.

properties.name: Modern Toilet Restaurant
properties.note: Weird toilet restaurant (Tony did not eat here)

properties.name: National Palace Museum
properties.note: Lots to look at but Tony came to look at the jade sculptures

properties.name: No. 16, Lane 38, Section 3, Zhinan Road
properties.note: This used to be the Yuan Xu Yuan teahouse he visited. Now Closed.

properties.name: No. 60, Fuguo Road
properties.note: He Xiang Delicious. Bamboo shoot soup with lotus leaves and savory dumplings. Lotus Rice Parcel: Steamed sticky rice with salted duck egg and pork, served with a sweet red sauce

properties.name: Raohe Night Market
properties.note: Large market selling wares and food

properties.name: Spring City Shrimp Fishing Restaurant
properties.note: Catch your own shrimp and they cook it for you

properties.name: Taipei 101
properties.note: One of the tallest buildings in the world. Check out the view.

properties.name: VILLA 32
properties.note: Hot Spring resort. If you've got the dough, a decidedly posh level of Zen-like relaxation. Only 4 rooms so book way in advance.

properties.name: W Taipei
properties.note: Hotel

properties.name: WOOBAR
properties.note: cocktails

properties.name: Ximen Mango Shaved Ice
properties.note: Shaved ice with mango. This isn't the exact location but Tony says visit any shaved ice place, they are ubiquitous in Taiwan.

properties.name: Xinglong Park
properties.note: Tony watched a form of tai chi that includes sparring (called pushing hands).

properties.name: Yongkang Street
properties.note: Laidback area with great restaurants.

properties.name: 櫻崗溫泉會館 (Sakuraoka Hot Springs Resort)
properties.note: Nice views and attentive staff

properties.name: andBeyond Ngorongoro Crater Lodge
properties.note: "Idyllic natural setting and good plumbing? Pretty much paradise," Tony describes.

properties.name: Arusha
properties.note: Tony flew to this small town before heading into the Serengheti.

properties.name: Lake Magadi
properties.note: They had lunch by the lake after driving around looking at zebras, hyena, lions, giraffe, and elephants. There was pesto pasta and brownies involved.

properties.name: Ndutu Camp
properties.note: Tony and wildlife biologist visited this area where tribal people called Maasai people live and share land with the lions

properties.name: Ngorongoro Crater
properties.note: "An entire ecosystem within an ecosystem" Tony said of this volcano crater. He had breakfast at his hotel overlooking the crater lake.

properties.name: Forodhani Gardens
properties.note: Street foods galore! Tony had octopus and masala lobster with chili mango, grilled on a skewer. He also had Zanzibar pizza (beef, tomato, onion, cheese, egg, and mayo rolled up in a dough and fried)

properties.name: Jambiani
properties.note: Tony visited this coastal town, which was an important site to raise the support of revolution for equal rights. A family cooked a meal for him. Coconut rice, freshly caught tasi fish, simmer with garlic and lime, topped with umngqusho (a fresh salad of chopped tomato, eggplant, cucumber, potato), and another fish which is a type of mackeral, marinated in lime and garlic and fried. Served with various breads, cassava, and rice.

properties.name: Jaw's Corner
properties.note: Tony had coffee and a pastry at an outdoor cafe here

properties.name: Stone Town
properties.note: Somewhere on the outskirts of Stone Town, Tony visited a vendor that specializes in meats on skewers. He had beef on a stick, chicken (prepared with garlic, lime, coriander, ginger, salt, and pepper), tamarind-chili sauce, fries.

properties.name: Amphawa
properties.note: A floating market where Tony ate fried shrimp cakes and a mixed salad plate.

properties.name: Likhit Kai Yang
properties.note: Tony ate isan style chicken and green papaya salad, which he described as so hot, "I think my pubes just caught on fire."

properties.name: Nefertiti
properties.note: Coffee and hookah

properties.name: Seafood Market and Restaurant
properties.note: Mega seafood market with food stands and tables. You pick your fish, chilis, ingredients, and how they cook it, they serve it to you.

properties.name: Moo Sa-Tae Nai Aood
properties.note: Food stand serving ginger and pork sausage

properties.name: Chang Phuak Gate Night Market - Food Stalls
properties.note: Known as the Khao Ka Moo Cowboy Hat Lady, where they ate Khao kha moo (stewed pork leg).

properties.name: Chiang Mai
properties.note: Tony met Andy Ricker (Pok Pok restaurants) in a small village in this region.

properties.name: Chiangmai Cabaret show
properties.note: Ladyboy cabaret show

properties.name: Gia Tod Teing Keun
properties.note: Tony and Andy had deep fried chicken and fish, Thai chili dip nam prik noom, Thai pork sausage

properties.name: Khao Soi Lam Duan
properties.note: For breakfast, Tony had Khao soi (curry coconut milk noodle soup), som tam (green papaya salad), Thai iced tea.

properties.name: Krapao
properties.note: Andy and Tony ate Pad kee mao (drunken noodles) here, a spicy dish that has no noodles for some reason. They also ate kra pao gai (Thai basil chicken), pad cha talay (seafood stirfry).

properties.name: Laap Kao Cham Chaa
properties.note: Tony and Andy ate fried frog with crunchy fried garlic.

properties.name: Sak Yant Tattoo
properties.note: Tony and Andy both got sak yant tattoos (traditional Thai tattoos)

properties.name: Zoom Bar
properties.note: Tony and Andy had grilled pork intestine and drinks.

properties.name: Aroon Rai
properties.note: Northern Thai food, very spicy

properties.name: Him Tang Pig's Blood Soup
properties.note: Tony and Andy Ricker visited this small but popular restaurant. They had grilled pig tail, Northern Thai herbal pork sausage, pig's brain, and pig's blood soup.

properties.name: Mae Klong Railway Market
properties.note: A market made on top of an active railroad. They have to move and pause the market every time the train comes through.

properties.name: Pa Daeng Jin Tup

They had Lebanese food here: "baharat spiced beef and lamb kabobs, fried cauliflower with tahini, a summer fatteh with local squash and charred tomatoes topped with sizzling brown butter and pine nuts; sweet and sour rice wrapped in grape leaves; hummus."

properties.name: Melrose Market
properties.note: Artisanal food vendors

properties.name: North Star Diner & Shanghai Room
properties.note: Tony met with retired Seattle homicide detective Mike Ciesynski to discuss serial killers. They ordered food from North Star Diner: crab, bacon, and melted cheese sandwich

properties.name: Ocho
properties.note: Tony met with rock star Mark Lanegan (from Queens of the Stone Age, and also the person who wrote the theme show of Parts Unknown). They had tapas and drinks.

properties.name: Pacific Inn Pub
properties.note: Tony had fish & chips with Astra Elane and Dustin Patterson, members of local band The Gods Themselves.

properties.name: Paseo Fremont
properties.note: The Cuban pork roast sandwich has a cult following.

properties.name: Pike Place Market
properties.note: Famous market with the fish throwers and other vendors

properties.name: Piroshky Piroshky
properties.note: Russian pies filled with potatoes, beef, or cabbage

properties.name: Quinn's Pub
properties.note: Pub with burgers and wild boar sloppy joes

properties.name: Rainier Restaurant and BBQ
properties.note: Gator or rattlesnake. Wok seared beef. Deep fried quail. Tamarind seafood soup.

properties.name: Revel
properties.note: Tony ate at this Korean restaurant with John Cook and Todd Bishop, co-founders of tech news website GeekWire. They had: "cilantro noodles with yu choy, and thinly sliced flat iron steak; kimchi pancakes with braised pork belly and bean sprouts; marinated short rib rice bowl with house sambal daikon, kimchi, and egg; and short rib dumplings topped with pickled shallot and scallion salad."

properties.name: Salty's on Alki Beach
properties.note: Fish and chips

properties.name: Salumi
properties.note: Porchetta, Meatball, Finocchiano. Come early because they sell out

properties.name: Seatown Market Diner
properties.note: Crab avocado breakfast sandwich

properties.name: Seattle Ferry Terminal
properties.note: Take a ferry to one of the surrounding islands

properties.name: Shorty's
properties.note: Bar with pinball and hot dogs.

properties.name: Sitka and Spruce
properties.note: High quality produce-driven dishes. Locally grown veggies from their farm on Vashon Island

properties.name: Sun Liquor Distillery
properties.note: Bar makes its own gin and squeezes fresh juice

properties.name: Taylor Shellfish Oyster Bar
properties.note: Tony ate with a group. They had lots of oysters; flash-fried whole local smelt served with pepperoncini sambal aioli; local dungeness crab, chilled and cooked with pickled ginger sauce.

properties.name: The Calf & Kid
properties.note: Cheesemongers. Local cheeses

properties.name: The Edgewater Hotel
properties.note: Hosted the Beatles. Very "Twin Peaks"

properties.name: The Walrus and the Carpenter
properties.note: Oyster bar and casual restaurant. Fried oysters with cilantro aioli. Prawn crudo.

properties.name: Tilikum Place Cafe
properties.note: Dutch baby. Cross between a souffle and a pancake. Crispy on outside, soft in the middle

properties.name: Shelburne Falls Bowling Alley
properties.note: The second oldest bowling alley in the US, where pin bowling is the game.

properties.name: The Hollingsworth Cannabis Company
properties.note: A marijuana farm that supplies some of the dispensaries

properties.name: Aqueduct Racetrack
properties.note: Tony met with his friend and author Laurie Woolever, who co-wrote his cookbook Appetites with him. They bet on some horses and ate Jamaican beef patties.

properties.name: Big Bend National Park
properties.note: steak with green onion, grilled corn, tortillas, beans

properties.name: Shady Glen
properties.note: Old school diner specializing in many pies. Tony had corned beef and cabbage and raspberry cream pie.

properties.name: Kalaeloa Harbor
properties.note: Tony went diving with a fisherman for some octopus off the coast of Moloka'i, which they hunted with the spear.

properties.name: Keawanui Fishpond
properties.note: A sacred place in the community on Moloka'i, which is known as "The Friendly Place," but has a reputation for being unwelcoming to outsiders on other islands. They had slow roasted pig, grilled kala fish, mullet cooked lavalu style, octopus, poi, and freshwater clams from the mountains.

properties.name: Rondout Bay Cafe & Marina
properties.note: Working man's bar on the marina.

properties.name: Town
properties.note: Tony had pan roasted mahi mahi in this local seafood restaurant

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had beef, fried chiles, and biscuits with the Means family on their ranch.

properties.name: Vashon Island
properties.note: Tony ate with local chefs. They had a "clam bake with dungeness crab, spot prawns, corn, and potatoes, covered in kelp and steamed together under burlap."

properties.name: Santa Monica Farmers Market
properties.note: Fresh produce

visits.title: Los Angeles, My Own Heart of Darkness

properties.name: Rusty Star BBQ
properties.note: Closed now, but this used to be the Rusty Star BBQ, where Tony ate barbecue with Ted Nugent

properties.name: Tasty Crust Restaurant
properties.note: Tony met with a Hawaiian journalist, Daniel Ikaikaito. They had a plate lunch, a Hawaiian specialty of a protein with white rice and potato-mac salad. They ate theirs with chicken katsu, hamburger steak in “sinister” gravy, seared ahi with nori and sesame seed.

properties.name: 3205 K St NW
properties.note: This used to be Chadwicks, where Tony had beers and burgers with a former US spy

properties.name: Ben's Chili Bowl
properties.note: Beloved institution in DC that serves chili dogs

properties.name: District Wharf
properties.note: Chesapeake Bay crabs

properties.name: minibar by José Andrés
properties.note: "Big daddy of the DC restaurant scene," says Tony of Jose Andres

properties.name: Brooksrun Mining Co
properties.note: Tony ate bear meat in a coal mine with miners.

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony had dinner with the McKinney family, “a typical expression of hard-scrabble Appalachian practicality,” combined with “Neapolitan roots”: spaghetti pizza, chicken stew with peas and onions, pumpkin pie cake.

properties.name: Mount View High School
properties.note: Tony visited this high school where football is life. Tony attends the pre-game meal of ribs, baked potatoes, and chicken.

properties.name: The Pit Authentic Barbecue
properties.note: Tony visited Mitchell's BBQ which is now closed. This is the newer spot.

properties.name: Belle Isle Seafood
properties.note: Clam chowder, fried clams, fried shrimp, lobster, onion rings, fried haddock, etc. Lobster rolls.

properties.name: Neir's Tavern
properties.note: Tony had a beer with Loycent “Loy” Gordon, the owner of the tavern, and New York City Fire Department lieutenant. The movie "Goodfellas" filmed scenes in this bar.

properties.name: X2O Xaviars on the Hudson
properties.note: Tony ate lunch with Bill Murray here

properties.name: The French Laundry
properties.note: Tony was extremely excited to eat at this restaurant, called Thomas Keller "the top of his game" and best chef at that time. Tony eats with Michael Ruhlman, Eric Ripert, and Scott Bryan (who owned Veritas, which was one of Tony's favorite NY restaurants)

properties.name: Cantina del Vigía
properties.note: Tony and friends ate a feast of empanadas with corn and shredded, slow-cooked beef; goat cheese and spinach croquettes; fried fish; provoleta (bubbling cheese that “puffs up and inflates on the plate”); whole roasted suckling wild boar; whole roasted fish; beef milanesa covered in tomato sauce, ham, and shredded cheese.

properties.name: Garzon
properties.note: World famous restauranteur Francis Mallmann has a restaurant here. They eat at a big bonfire: salt crusted pork, pumpkin, fried bread (torta frita), veggies cooked in hot stone, and a pascualina which is a tart with pastry and eggs

properties.name: Las Nenas Steak House
properties.note: Tony, Nacho, and Diego went to this drive through to have skirt steak and ribeye, french fries, blood sausages, and beer..

properties.name: Parador La Huella
properties.note: Tony ate with the owners of this place. They had whole fish baked in salt, rib-eye steak, flank steak, suckling pig.

properties.name: Bar Arocena
properties.note: Unofficial sandwich of Uruguay: the Chivito: a steak, ham, bacon, cheese, hard boiled egg, mayo, and garnishes.

properties.name: Bar Las Flores
properties.note: Tony had pizza with the Hablan por la Espalda bandmates. They had pizza a caballo, or “a bizarro stoner hybrid topped with thin chickpea flatbread”

properties.name: Chiviteria Marcos
properties.note: Chivito sandwich, you pick your toppings. It is ENORMOUS. Tony ate here with his brother

properties.name: Escaramuza Libros
properties.note: Tony had lunch with journalist Carina Novarese. They had homemade sausage with roasted turnip and fermented vegetables, beet quiche.

properties.name: Estancia del Puerto
properties.note: This is the parillas stand that Tony and his brother Chris ate at

properties.name: Jacinto
properties.note: Tony had dinner with chef and owner Lucía Soria and Nacho. They had "homemade gnocchi with mushrooms and pumpkin; prawns with avocado, apple, and wasabi; beef tartare with quail egg."

properties.name: Mercado del Puerto
properties.note: Lots of food stands with lots of parillas (grilled meats)

properties.name: At The Arkadiy Cafe
properties.note: Drink tea

properties.name: Bánh Cuốn Nóng Kim Thoa
properties.note: Tony met with Thao Griffiths, the country director of the Vietnam Foundation. They ate Banh cuon, a dish of ground pork with wood ear mushroom in a thin rice noodle roll that is dipped in broth.

properties.name: Bia Hải Xồm
properties.note: Tony met with his old friend Dinh Hoang Linh, his former Foreign Affairs Ministry minder. "Bia Hảirefers to the roadside joints where locals gather to enjoy keg dispensed, freshly brewed draft beer

properties.name: Bún chả Hương Liên
properties.note: Tony met with President Obama (while he was in office) to have bún chả. It was Obama's first visit to Vietnam, and it reminded him of his childhood visits to Indonesia. They shared cold beers and bún chả, a traditional Vietnamese soup of grilled pork belly, pork patties, and noodles served in a broth of vinegar, sugar, fish sauce and served with chilies. Obama told a story about a city in Indonesia called Puncak where he would eat at a roadside restaurant overlooking the tea fields, with a river running through the restaurant. You could pick a fish and they'd fry it and serve it with a bed …

properties.name: Bún chả Nem rán B2 Vĩnh Hồ
properties.note: Bun Cha (noodle dish with grilled meats)

properties.name: Bún Chửi Ngô Sĩ Liên
properties.note: Also known as "Cussing Noodles" due to the owner yelling at people, but is Tony's friend Ha Pham's favorite place to get bún chin zou, or as Tony describes, "a glorious steaming bowl of rice noodles with spicy chilies, a rich hearty porky broth with pig's knuckles and snout. It's the only item on the menu and it's good!"

properties.name: Bún ốc Giang phố cổ
properties.note: Tony started this Vietnam journey at this bún ốc restaurant in Hanoi's Old Quarter. Bún ốc is a snail noodle soup

properties.name: Hanoi
properties.note: Tony visited during Tet, the celebration of the Lunar New Year. He ate at many stands that were put up to celebrate the end of a fast.

properties.name: Hanoi Food Stand
properties.note: "The dish I crave above all others," Tony says of pho. This episode was a clip show showing "food porn" examples.

properties.name: Nhà Hàng Thái Hưng Lâu
properties.note: Chicken with Chinese herb broth, served with fried baguette

properties.name: 84a Nguyễn Du
properties.note: Not sure if this still exists, but this address was the Thu Vien Biblioteque Cafe where Tony drank snake wine and ate lots of French-inspired Vietnamese food.

visits.title: Cobra Heart - Foods That Make You Manly

properties.name: Banh Xeo 46A
properties.note: Tony has Vietnamese coffee and crepes, which are made with rice flour and coconut milk (banh xao), served with shrimp, pork, saffron, and bean sprouts.

visits.title: Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: Ben Thanh Market
properties.note: Breakfast in the market: Hoi An noodles, slices of pork with pork skin, pickled garlic

visits.title: Cobra Heart - Foods That Make You Manly
Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: Buffet Hương Rừng 1
properties.note: "Flavors of the forest" as in they serve all kinds of animals. Tony famously ate a still beating cobra heart here.

visits.title: Cobra Heart - Foods That Make You Manly

properties.name: Cháo mực Thanh Sơn
properties.note: Chao muc is squid soup

visits.title: Cobra Heart - Foods That Make You Manly

properties.name: Cơm Niêu Sài Gòn
properties.note: Tony's friend Mom Gow had passed away by the time he visited. "Even the colors seem more muted now. The atmosphere different."

visits.title: Cobra Heart - Foods That Make You Manly
Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: Quán Ốc Chi Mập
properties.note: Butter snails with chiles, freshwater crabs

visits.title: Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: The Lunch Lady (Nguyen Thi Thanh)
properties.note: Fresh ingredients from the market, world famous for her soups

visits.title: Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: Bánh Mì Phượng
properties.note: Delicious banh mi with liver pate, cucumbers, fresh basil, and a crispy fried egg

visits.title: Vietnam: There's No Place Like Home

properties.name: Hạ Long Bay
properties.note: Tony took a boat ride aboard the Emeraude to this bay, one of Vietnam's most visited destinations. "Hạ Long means 'where the dragon descends into the sea' and legend says that this is where a great dragon charged, protecting Vietnam from foreign invaders."

properties.name: Dong Ba Market
properties.note: Tony had bún bò Huế (bone broth with lemongrass), fermented shrimp paste with rice noodles, tender beef shank, crabmeat dumplings, pig’s foot; huyết (blood cake)

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony ate at Boi Tran’s home and gallery with Boi Tran, painter; Nguyen Qui Duc; Hương Lan, writer and food blogger; art collector Jean-François Hubert, and Philippe Damas. They had bird’s nest soup, made of swallow’s nests and served with crabmeat, crab roe, red onion, pepper, and seasoned steamed lotus seeds; lobster with red onion, ginger, lemongrass, and chiles, cooked and presented in a bowl with lime leaves and the stock poured over it

properties.name: Nhà hàng Duyên Anh
properties.note: Tony met with his old friend Dinh Hoang Linh and had grilled eel with lemongrass, green chili, and pepper. And lobster

properties.name: Quán bánh O Lé
properties.note: Tony had lunch here: bánh xèo (rice-flour pancakes), steamed and topped with fried shallots and cassava paste and crumbled fried shrimp; bánh bột lọc (cassava or tapioca with pork and shrimp wrapped in a banana leaf); fried sticky-rice dumplings filled with shrimp

properties.name: Meal with locals
properties.note: Tony visited this small village in northern Laos. The villagers cooked him chicken and veggies.
